item.title: Lavizzara flood: hazard zones plan approved
item.description: The Ticino State Council has approved the hazard zones plan for Lavizzara after the flood on 29-30 June 2024
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Lavizzara hazard plan approved on April 22
- 7 dead and 1 missing in the 2024 flood
- Technical analysis for future scenarios

## Key facts
- **What**: Hazard zones plan for Lavizzara
- When: April 22, 2025
- Where: Municipality of Lavizzara, Ticino
- Who: Ticino State Council
- Amount: Not specified

On April 22, the Ticino government adopted the Hazard Zones Plan for the Municipality of Lavizzara, concerning the phenomenon of flooding and riverbank erosion of the Peccia and Maggia rivers. The Vallemaggia has returned to normal, but the images of what happened in the night between June 29 and 30, 2024, are still vivid in many people's minds, when a downpour turned the life of an entire community upside down, destroying the territory and causing the death of seven people, while a young man from the valley is still missing.

Immediately after the tragedy in Vallemaggia, there was an extraordinary mobilization to help the community. The authorities moved to prevent a similar situation from recurring. In November 2025, the Department of Territory presented to the population of the Municipality of Lavizzara the hazard maps of the watercourses of Val Lavizzara and Val di Peccia affected by the tragic flood of June 29-30, 2024.

### Technical analysis and future scenarios

To the inhabitants of the Municipality of Lavizzara, the Canton wrote on November 14, 'the results of the technical analyses, historical research, and the numerous on-site inspections carried out throughout this year have been illustrated, which have made it possible to define future hazard scenarios, both for the lateral streams and for the Peccia and Maggia rivers.' The reconstruction of what happened and the hazard maps 'represent a fundamental element for defining the final restoration and reconstruction interventions, whose design is already underway, as well as for adapting the territorial planning to the risk scenarios highlighted and to perfect the Communal Emergency Plan.'

Now this hazard zones plan has been adopted by the State Council, as published in today's Official Gazette, referring to a decision of the executive dated April 22. In detail, the Official Gazette states that the Government has adopted 'the Hazard Zones Plan of the Municipality of Lavizzara, concerning the phenomenon of flooding and riverbank erosion of the Peccia river (Sant'Antonio and Piano di Peccia), the Lavizzara river (Mogno) and the Maggia river (Prato Sornico, Lovàlt and Broglio) and the phenomenon of debris flow of the slope watercourses in the locality of Sant'Antonio (Corona di Corte and Öir), Piano di Peccia (Sovénat and San Carlo), Mogno (Ri di Röd and Mognòla) and Prato-Sornico (Ri Scudau and Bosco di Casa).'

Against the adoption decision, 'the right of appeal to the Cantonal Administrative Court, Lugano, is granted within the term of 30 days. The appeal has no suspensive effect.'

A dramatic summer

The summer of 2024, we remember, was characterized by two violent downpours, which occurred a week apart. Before Vallemaggia, in fact, on the evening of June 21, the bad weather hit the Mesolcina, devastating the territory and causing the death of three people. ### Implications for the Community and the Territory

The approval of the hazard zone plan represents a fundamental step for the safety and planning of Lavizzara's territory. The technical analyses and on-site inspections carried out have made it possible to precisely identify the risk areas and define the necessary interventions to prevent future disasters.

### Comparison with the Previous Situation

Before the flood, Vallemaggia did not have a detailed plan of the hazard zones. The tragedy of June 29-30, 2024, highlighted the need for a more robust emergency plan and better territorial planning. The hazard maps now approved represent a significant step forward in managing hydrogeological risk.

### Future Scenarios and Planned Interventions

The approved plan includes restoration and reconstruction interventions, as well as adjustments to territorial planning to adapt to the risk scenarios highlighted. These interventions aim to prevent future floods and protect the local community. The authorities have already started designing the final interventions, which will be implemented in the coming months.

### Community Involvement

The population of Lavizzara has been involved from the beginning in the process of defining the hazard zone plan. The authorities have presented the results of the technical and historical-geological analyses, involving the community in planning the interventions. This participatory approach is fundamental to ensuring that the measures adopted are effective and accepted by the local population.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ### What to do now: procedures and deadlines

With the approval of the hazard zone plan, it is important that the community of Lavizzara is aware of the measures adopted and the procedures to follow in case of emergency. The authorities have published the details of the plan in the Official Gazette, which include the risk areas and preventive measures.

### Appeal to the Cantonal Administrative Court

An appeal against the decision to adopt the hazard zone plan can be made to the Cantonal Administrative Court of Lugano within a 30-day deadline. The appeal does not have a suspensive effect, meaning that the plan will come into force even in the event of an appeal.

### Next steps and interventions

The authorities have already started the design of the final restoration and reconstruction interventions. These interventions aim to prevent future floods and protect the local community. Territorial planning will be adapted to the risk scenarios highlighted, and the Municipal Emergency Plan will be improved.

### Useful tools

For further information and to plan your actions, consult the [hydrogeological risk calculator](nav:calculator) and the [insurance comparator](nav:health). These tools will help you better understand the risks and take the necessary measures to protect your property and your family.

*Source: [ticinonews.ch](https://www.ticinonews.ch/ticino/alluvione-in-lavizzara-il-consiglio-di-stato-approva-i-piani-delle-zone-di-pericolo-432480)*

item.title: Overtourism in Switzerland: Lucerne and Grindelwald collapsing
item.description: Lucerne surpasses Venice in tourist overnight stays. Initiative to block new hotels and Airbnb restrictions.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Lucerne surpasses Venice with 1.5 million overnight stays in 2024
- Initiative to block new hotels in Lucerne
- Restrictions on Airbnb in Ticino, Zurich, Lucerne, and Geneva

## Key facts
- **What**: Initiative to block new hotels in Lucerne
- When: From March 2026
- Where: Lucerne
- Who: Red-green circles
- Amount: 6,300 current hotel beds

In Switzerland, the debate on tourist accommodation has reached a breaking point. While a lack of visitors was once a concern, it is now the excess that is causing dissatisfaction. From Lucerne to Grindelwald, to the small Iseltwald, managing flows on peak days has become an unsustainable challenge.

In Lucerne, tension is palpable. With 1.5 million overnight stays in 2024, the city has set a new historic record, surpassing pre-pandemic volumes. The NZZ newspaper highlighted a symbolic figure: the ratio of tourists to inhabitants in Lucerne is now higher than that of Venice. The political response: an initiative to block new hotels.

Since the end of March 2026, a signature collection has been underway in Lucerne for a popular initiative promoted by red-green circles. The goal is to freeze hotel beds, currently at around 6,300. The proposal provides for an iron rule: the opening of a new facility will only be granted in exchange for the closure of another of the same category. This is not the first time the city has reacted: in 2017, citizens voted to eliminate a bus parking lot in the center, a clear signal against the invasion of tourist buses that had already then raised the issue of overtourism.

### Impact on the real estate market

One of the most critical issues is the impact of tourism on the real estate market. In iconic destinations like Zermatt or St. Moritz, to counteract the phenomenon, several cities have introduced restrictions on short-term rentals: Ticino: rental limit set at a maximum of 90 days per year. Zurich: the municipal council recently supported a similar proposal. Lucerne and Geneva: implementation of regulations to limit platforms such as Airbnb. A study by Switzerland Tourism confirms that, although the sector is perceived as positive for the economy, residents suffer from rising prices, overcrowding of transport, and pollution.

### Grindelwald: an amusement park

At the foot of Eiger, Mönch, and Jungfrau, the historic destination of Grindelwald lives a deep contradiction. Overnight stays have exploded, rising from 1.1 million in 2012 to over 1.6 million today. This success, however, has brought traffic and congestion, pushing many residents to feel like extras in a theme park.

### Iseltwald: the selfie tourism phenomenon

The phenomenon of 'selfie tourism' has also hit tiny and unprepared places. Iseltwald, on Lake Brienz, has become a worldwide pilgrimage site after the success of a South Korean series on Netflix: today, taking a photo on the famous pier costs 5 francs. Overtourism is having a significant impact on the quality of life for residents in Switzerland, particularly in the most popular tourist areas such as Lucerne and Grindelwald. The rise in property prices and short-term rentals is making it difficult for locals to find affordable housing. Additionally, overcrowding on public transport and roads is causing congestion and daily discomfort.

### Restrictions on Airbnb

Restrictions on Airbnb and other short-term rental platforms are a direct response to the increase in property prices and the scarcity of housing for residents. In Ticino, the rental limit set at 90 days per year is a measure aimed at reducing the impact of tourism on the local real estate market. Zurich, Lucerne, and Geneva have followed suit, implementing similar regulations to limit the use of these platforms.

### Environmental Impact

The increase in tourism also has a significant impact on the environment. Overcrowding on public transport and roads is causing an increase in pollution and congestion. Furthermore, the rise in the number of tourists is putting a strain on the natural resources of tourist areas, such as water and energy.

### Local Community Reactions

The reactions of the local community to overtourism are varied. Some residents are concerned about the impact on their lifestyle and quality of life. Others see tourism as an economic opportunity and are in favor of measures to better manage tourist flows. However, there is a growing consensus that a balance needs to be found between tourism and the quality of life for residents.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). For cross-border workers living and working in Switzerland, overtourism can have a significant impact on their daily lives. The increase in property prices and short-term rentals can make it difficult to find affordable housing. Additionally, overcrowding on public transport and roads can cause discomfort and delays in daily commutes.

### What to do

If you are a cross-border worker living in Switzerland, there are some measures you can take to mitigate the impact of overtourism on your daily life. First, try to plan your trips in advance to avoid peak hours and periods of high tourist influx. Additionally, consider using alternative means of transport, such as bicycles or less crowded public transport.

### Useful tools

To help you manage your daily life in Switzerland better, you can use tools like the [cost of living calculator](nav:cost-of-living) and the rental comparator. These tools can help you find affordable housing and plan your expenses better.

### Conclusion

Overtourism is a complex problem that requires careful management and collaboration between local authorities, residents, and tourists. For cross-border workers, it is important to be aware of the impact of tourism on their daily lives and take measures to mitigate these effects. By using tools like the cost of living calculator and the rental comparator, you can find practical solutions to live better in Switzerland.

*Source: [comozero.it](https://comozero.it/turismo/overtourism-svizzera-lucerna-grindelwald-crisi-alloggi/)*

item.title: Varese Hospital: Closures for May 1st and San Vittore
item.description: Changes to healthcare service hours for the May 1st bank holiday and San Vittore's patronal feast in Varese.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Closure of the Ospedale di Circolo Collection Point on May 2
- General closure on May 8 for San Vittore
- Suspended services: CUP, collections, retrieval of reports, administrative offices

## Key facts
- **What**: Changes to healthcare service hours
- When: May 2 and 8, 2026
- Where: Ospedale di Circolo di Varese and ASST Sette Laghi territorial sites
- Who: ASST Sette Laghi
- Amount: Not specified

On the occasion of the upcoming spring bank holiday and the feast of San Vittore, the patron saint of Varese, ASST Sette Laghi has announced some changes to the opening hours of collection points and administrative offices. The week of changes to healthcare service hours in the area will begin on Saturday, May 2, with the closure of the Collection Point at Varese's Ospedale di Circolo, located in the Santa Maria Pavilion. The service will resume regularly the following week.

### General closure for San Vittore

The most significant closure will concern Friday, May 8, the day on which San Vittore, the patron saint of Varese, is celebrated. On this date, the closure will be general and will affect all company sites, from Luino to Tradate. The services suspended for the entire Friday include:
- CUP and Outpatient Clinic Secretariats
- Collection Points and report retrieval desks
- Administrative Reception and URP
- District Offices and territorial sites

The company specifies that the shutdown on May 8 involves the entire territorial network of ASST and not just the facilities in the provincial capital. As always in these cases, emergencies and ward activities remain guaranteed.

### Implications for patients

The closures scheduled for May 2 and 8 may cause inconvenience to patients who need blood tests or to collect reports. It is advisable to book any tests in advance or contact your doctor to check the availability of alternative services. For emergencies, emergency services and ward activities remain operational. ## Analysis of Closures and Alternative Services

The closures scheduled for the May 1st bank holiday and for the San Vittore holiday could have a significant impact on patients who need healthcare services. In particular, the closure of the Blood Drawing Center at the Circolo Hospital in Varese on May 2nd could cause inconvenience to those who need blood tests or to deliver samples. It is important to plan ahead and, if possible, book the tests on the days before or after.

### Alternative Services

For those who need to pick up test results or access administrative services, it is possible to contact their doctor or the clinic's office to check the availability of alternative services. Additionally, you can use the online services of ASST Sette Laghi to book tests or access test results.

### Comparisons with Other Holidays

The closures scheduled for May 2nd and 8th follow a similar pattern to other national and local holidays. Generally, during these days, non-essential healthcare services are suspended to ensure the rest of the staff and the management of emergencies. However, it is important to note that the closures on May 2nd only affect the Blood Drawing Center of the Circolo Hospital, while those on May 8th are generalized and affect all company locations.

### Practical Scenarios

For patients who need blood tests on May 2nd, it is advisable to book the tests on the days before or after. For those who need to pick up test results or access administrative services, it is possible to contact their doctor or the clinic's office to check the availability of alternative services. Additionally, you can use the online services of ASST Sette Laghi to book tests or access test results.

### Conclusion

The closures scheduled for the May 1st bank holiday and for the San Vittore holiday could cause inconvenience to patients, but with good planning, it is possible to minimize the inconveniences. It is important to contact your doctor or the clinic's office to check the availability of alternative services and use the online services of ASST Sette Laghi to book tests or access test results.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## What to do in case of closures

In case of closures of healthcare services, it is important to follow some procedures to minimize inconveniences. Here's what to do:

### Book exams in advance

If you need blood tests or to deliver samples, it is advisable to book the exams on the days before or after the closure. Contact your doctor or the clinic's reception to check the availability of alternative services.

### Use online services

ASST Sette Laghi offers a series of online services that can be used to book exams or access reports. Visit the ASST Sette Laghi website for more information on the available services.

### Contact your doctor

If you need to collect reports or access administrative services, contact your doctor or the clinic's reception to check the availability of alternative services. Your doctor can provide you with information on the available services and how to access them.

### Use emergency services

In case of emergency, emergency room services and ward activities remain operational. If you need immediate assistance, go to the nearest emergency room or contact the emergency number.

### Plan ahead

To avoid inconveniences, it is important to plan ahead. Check the dates of the closures and organize your exams and visits so as not to be caught off guard. Contact your doctor or the clinic's reception to check the availability of alternative services and use the online services of ASST Sette Laghi to book exams or access reports.

### Conclusion

The closures scheduled for the May 1st bank holiday and for the San Vittore holiday may cause inconveniences to patients, but with good planning, it is possible to minimize the inconveniences. Follow the indicated procedures and use the online services of ASST Sette Laghi to book exams or access reports. In case of emergency, emergency room services and ward activities remain operational.

Compare the [CHF/EUR exchange rate](nav:exchange) in real time across providers: save up to 1.5% on monthly transfer fees.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/ospedale-di-circolo-e-sedi-territoriali-le-chiusure-per-il-ponte-del-1-maggio-e-san-vittore/2561005/)*

item.title: Wellness district in the shadow of Campo dei Fiori
item.description: Discover how 2% of taxpayers hold 15% of the income in these hillside communes of Varese.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- 2% of taxpayers hold 15% of income
- Average income of 34,746 euros in the area
- Luvinate leads with an average income of 37,654 euros

## Key facts
- **What**: Declared average income
- When: 2024 declarations
- Where: Municipalities of the Varese hills
- Who: Taxpayers of the municipalities of Luvinate, Casciago, Barasso, Comerio, Galliate Lombardo, Ranco, Bodio Lomnago
- Amount: Weighted average income of 34,746 euros

In the 'well-being district' in the shadow of Campo dei Fiori, the municipalities of the Varese hills show a concentration of wealth above the provincial average. The municipalities of Luvinate, Casciago, Barasso, Comerio, Galliate Lombardo, Ranco, and Bodio Lomnago, with a total of 13,900 inhabitants and 10,548 taxpayers, present a fiscal identity that clearly emerges from the 2024 declarations published by the Ministry of Economy. Luvinate tops the ranking with an average declared income of 37,654 euros per taxpayer, closely followed by Galliate Lombardo with 37,585 euros. Comerio, Ranco, Casciago, Bodio Lomnago, and Barasso follow with average incomes of 34,551, 34,518, 34,039, 34,029, and 33,672 euros respectively. The weighted average income of the entire area is 34,746 euros, 24% higher than the provincial average.

The most significant data is the concentration of the upper part of the income distribution. Out of the 10,548 taxpayers in the area, 237 declare over 120,000 euros in total income, equal to 2.33% of the total, compared to 0.97% of the provincial average. In some municipalities, such as Galliate Lombardo, Casciago, and Barasso, the percentage of taxpayers with incomes over 120,000 euros rises to 3.55%, 3.35%, and 3.20% respectively. These 237 taxpayers alone generate 51 million euros in total income, equal to 14.5% of all income declared in the district.

### The mayors' statements
The mayors of the involved municipalities comment on the data with caution. The mayor of Casciago, Mirko Reto, emphasizes the attractiveness of the territory: 'Casciago is a jewel nestled between the lake and Campo dei Fiori. The high income is related to the country's ability to attract families and professionals seeking a high quality of life, efficient services, and a strategic position between Varese and Gavirate. Casciago is part of a cohesive territorial system. Collaboration with neighboring municipalities such as Luvinate, Barasso, and Comerio creates a sort of 'excellence district' that benefits from common policies and a shared vision of the territory.'

The mayor of Comerio, Michele Ballarini, confirms that a wealthy municipality does not necessarily mean a wealthy administration, as the State cuts transfers. These data highlight how the wealth of residents does not automatically translate into resources for the municipalities, which must still guarantee high-quality services to maintain the attractiveness of the territory. ### Implications for cross-border workers
The data on average income and wealth concentration in the Varese hills municipalities can have various implications for cross-border workers who commute to Switzerland and reside in Italy. Firstly, the presence of a population segment with high incomes can influence the local real estate market, making the purchase or rental of properties more expensive. This can pose a challenge for cross-border workers seeking to settle in these areas, especially if their income is not high enough to compete with local residents.

### Comparison with the previous situation
Before the publication of this data, the perception of wealth in the area was based on anecdotal observations and not on official data. Now, with the 2024 declarations, it is possible to have a clearer picture of income distribution and local economic dynamics. This can help cross-border workers make more informed decisions about choosing a place of residence and better evaluate the economic opportunities offered by the area.

### Future scenarios
With the increasing concentration of wealth, it is possible that the municipalities of the Varese hills will attract more and more professionals and affluent families. This could lead to a further increase in the prices of real estate and services, making the area even more exclusive. However, it could also lead to an improvement in public services and infrastructure, thanks to the greater availability of financial resources. Cross-border workers will therefore need to closely monitor the evolution of the situation to adapt to the new market dynamics.

## Useful planning tools
To estimate your pension strategy, use the [pension planner](nav:pension) and the [pillar 3 simulator](nav:pillar3). ### What to do for cross-border workers
For cross-border workers considering moving to one of the municipalities in the well-being district, it's important to carefully evaluate your budget and needs. First, it's advisable to accurately estimate moving expenses and living costs, taking into account property prices and service costs in the area. Additionally, it's useful to gather information about job opportunities and local labor market conditions to ensure you find employment that meets your expectations.

### Transfer procedure
Moving to one of the municipalities in the well-being district requires some fundamental steps. First, you need to find suitable accommodation for your needs and budget. Subsequently, it's important to register with the municipality of residence and obtain all the necessary documents to live and work in Italy. This may include applying for a tax code, enrolling in the National Health Service, and registering with INPS.

### Useful tools
To facilitate the move and integration into the new community, cross-border workers can use various tools and resources. For example, you can use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to get an idea of expected expenses and income. Additionally, it's useful to consult the guides and resources available on the Frontaliere Ticino website, which provide detailed information on living and working in Ticino and neighboring areas.

### Final CTA
For more information and to best plan your move, use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) and consult our guides and resources. Don't hesitate to contact us for any questions or clarifications.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/nel-distretto-del-benessere-allombra-del-campo-dei-fiori-dove-il-2-dei-contribuenti-ha-il-15-del-reddito/2561317/)*

item.title: Moncucco Group: 120k hospitalizations, 187m revenue in 2025
item.description: The Moncucco Group closes 2025 with 120,000 hospitalizations and a revenue of 187 million, focusing on staff training.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- 120,000 hospitalizations in 2025
- Revenue of 187 million
- 300 employees in training

## Key facts
- What: Increase in hospitalizations
- When: 2025
- Where: Clinica Moncucco and Clinica Santa Chiara
- Who: Moncucco Group
- Amount: 120,000 hospitalizations
- What: Group revenue
- When: 2025
- Where: Ticino
- Who: Moncucco Group
- Amount: 187 million
- **What**: Employees in training
- When: 2025
- Where: Clinica Moncucco and Clinica Santa Chiara
- Who: Moncucco Group
- Amount: 300

The Moncucco Hospital Group closed 2025 with positive results, exceeding the threshold of 120,000 hospitalizations and recording revenue of 187 million francs. This increase compared to the 107,000 cases in 2024 was made possible by infrastructure, technological, and personnel investments. Director Christian Camponovo emphasized that the growth in activity is a sign of confidence from patients towards the group's two clinics, Clinica Moncucco and Clinica Santa Chiara. However, he also highlighted that the increase in care needs is linked to the aging population, a challenge that requires constant commitment to staff training.

### Investments and Training

The Moncucco Group has significantly invested in staff training, with 300 employees out of 952 in training. This commitment is crucial to address the shortage of qualified personnel and ensure generational change. The president of the Board of Directors, Mauro Dell'Ambrogio, emphasized that the group works at lower rates compared to others, with an average rate for stationary patients of 9,120 francs, compared to the 10,000 francs of the Ente Ospedaliero Cantonale.

### Acquisitions and Future Prospects

At the end of 2025, the Moncucco Group acquired the psychiatric services of the Viarnetto clinic, a further expansion that strengthens the group's presence in the Ticino healthcare sector. Mauro Dell'Ambrogio expressed pride in this acquisition, emphasizing the continuity with the tradition of quality of the Viarnetto clinic.

### Implications for Cross-Border Workers

The positive results of the Moncucco Group can have a significant impact on cross-border workers working in the healthcare sector in Ticino. The increase in hospitalizations and investments in staff training can offer new job and professional development opportunities. Furthermore, the reduction in rates compared to other hospital entities can positively influence healthcare costs for residents and cross-border workers. ## Analysis of Implications

The increase in hospitalizations and investments in the Moncucco Group offers various opportunities for cross-border workers employed in the healthcare sector in Ticino. The rise in training personnel and the acquisition of the Viarnetto clinic can create new job positions and career opportunities. Furthermore, the reduction in tariffs compared to other hospital entities can improve accessibility to healthcare services for residents and cross-border workers.

### Comparisons with the Previous Situation

Compared to 2024, the Moncucco Group has recorded a significant increase in hospitalizations, rising from 107,000 to 120,000 cases. This increase was made possible by infrastructural and technological investments, which have improved the group's response capacity. Additionally, the acquisition of the Viarnetto clinic represents an important step for the group's expansion in the psychiatric sector.

### Future Scenarios

With the aging population and the increase in care needs, the Moncucco Group faces new challenges. However, investments in staff training and the acquisition of new facilities can offer growth and development opportunities for cross-border workers in the healthcare sector. Moreover, the reduction in tariffs can improve accessibility to healthcare services, benefiting both residents and cross-border workers.

### Procedures and Required Documents

For cross-border workers interested in working for the Moncucco Group, it is important to know the procedures and required documents for hiring. Among these, the work permit, the residence permit, and identity documents are essential. Additionally, it is necessary to verify the specific requirements for open positions and follow the group's selection procedures.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## Actions and Procedures

For cross-border workers who wish to work at the Moncucco Group, it is important to follow some specific procedures. First, it is necessary to check the open positions on the group's website and submit your application. Once selected, candidates will need to provide identity documents, work permit, and residence permit. Additionally, it is important to follow the group's selection and training procedures to ensure a successful career.

### Deadlines and Required Documents

The deadlines for submitting applications and presenting documents vary depending on the open positions. It is important to check the specific deadlines on the group's website and submit the required documents within the established deadlines. Among the necessary documents, the work permit, residence permit, and identity documents are essential. Additionally, it is important to follow the group's selection and training procedures to ensure a successful career.

### Useful Tools and Resources

For cross-border workers who wish to work at the Moncucco Group, it is useful to use the tools and resources available on the group's website. Among these, the salary calculator, the health insurance comparator, and the work permit comparator can be useful for planning your career and ensuring a satisfying work and personal life.

[Calculate your salary](nav:calculator) to best plan your career in the healthcare sector.

### Conclusion

The Moncucco Group represents an important opportunity for cross-border workers who wish to work in the healthcare sector in Ticino. With the increase in hospitalizations and investments in staff training, the group offers new job and professional development opportunities. Additionally, the reduction in rates compared to other hospital entities can improve accessibility to healthcare services for residents and cross-border workers. By following the procedures and using the available tools, cross-border workers can ensure a successful career at the Moncucco Group.

*Source: [ticinonews.ch](https://www.ticinonews.ch/ticino/il-2025-sorride-al-gruppo-moncucco-sempre-piu-numeroso-432518)*

item.title: Conference in Milan on Italy-Switzerland anti-mafia cooperation
item.description: Fiorenza Bergomi, judge of the Federal Criminal Court, among the speakers at the conference on May 11, 2026 at the University of Milan
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Conference on the mafia and Italy-Switzerland cooperation on May 11, 2026 in Milan
- Fiorenza Bergomi, judge of the Federal Criminal Court, will participate
- Topic: legislation, investigations, and cross-border cooperation

## Key facts
- **What**: Conference on the mafia and Italy-Switzerland cooperation
- When: May 11, 2026
- Where: University of Milan
- Who: Fiorenza Bergomi, Nando Dalla Chiesa, Annamaria Astrologo, Pasquale Addesso, Giuseppe Furciniti
- Cost: Not yet specified

The conference 'The mafia beyond borders: legislation, investigations, and cooperation between Italy and Switzerland' will be held on Monday, May 11, 2026 at the University of Milan. Among the speakers will be Fiorenza Bergomi, judge of the Criminal Court of the Federal Criminal Court based in Bellinzona. Bergomi has also worked as a prosecutor at the Cantonal Ministry for several years. In addition to Bergomi, Nando Dalla Chiesa, Annamaria Astrologo, Pasquale Addesso, and Giuseppe Furciniti will speak. The conference will be moderated by Fabio Basile.

### Speakers and topics
The conference will feature experts from both countries. Nando Dalla Chiesa will speak about 'Organized crime and education in legality: institutions and territory'. Annamaria Astrologo, scientific coordinator of the Ticino Observatory on organized crime, will share her experience and knowledge of the phenomenon. Pasquale Addesso, deputy prosecutor of the District Anti-Mafia Directorate of Milan, and Giuseppe Furciniti, head of the Anti-Mafia Investigative Directorate Milan, will discuss investigations and strategies to combat organized crime.

### Importance of cross-border cooperation
Cooperation between Italy and Switzerland is fundamental to combat organized crime that operates across borders. The conference represents an opportunity to share experiences, knowledge, and effective strategies. Fiorenza Bergomi, with her experience both in Switzerland and in Ticino, will make a significant contribution to the discussion.

### Implications for cross-border workers
Cross-border workers who work in Switzerland and reside in Italy, or vice versa, may be particularly interested in the topics covered in the conference. The fight against the mafia and cross-border cooperation can influence the security and stability of the region, making the border safer for everyone.

### Conference program
The detailed program of the conference has not yet been specified, but it is expected that there will be discussion sessions, presentations, and debates between the speakers and the public. The event will be held at the University of Milan, an institution renowned for its research and studies on organized crime.

### Conclusion
The conference on May 11, 2026 represents an important opportunity to deepen knowledge and cooperation between Italy and Switzerland in the fight against the mafia. The participation of experts from both countries underscores the importance of effective cross-border collaboration to ensure security and legality in the region. ### Analysis of Cross-Border Challenges
The fight against organized crime across borders requires close collaboration between the authorities of the two countries. The Milan conference represents an opportunity to discuss the challenges and opportunities of this cooperation. Fiorenza Bergomi, with her experience in the Swiss judicial system, can offer a unique perspective on the difficulties and possible solutions.

### Experiences and Best Practices
Annamaria Astrologo, scientific coordinator of the Ticino Observatory on Organized Crime, will bring her knowledge and experiences gained in Ticino. Her participation is crucial to understanding how organized crime operates in a region with a strong presence of cross-border workers.

### Investigative Strategies
Pasquale Addesso and Giuseppe Furciniti, with their experience in anti-mafia investigations, will discuss the most effective investigative strategies. Their presentations will be useful for understanding how Italian authorities tackle organized crime and how they can collaborate with Swiss authorities.

### Education in Legality
Nando Dalla Chiesa, delegate of the Rector of the University of Milan, will speak about the importance of education in legality. This topic is particularly relevant for young people and those living in areas with a strong presence of organized crime.

### Impact on Cross-Border Workers
Cross-border workers who work in Switzerland and reside in Italy, or vice versa, may be particularly interested in the topics discussed at the conference. The fight against the mafia and cross-border cooperation can influence the security and stability of the region, making the border safer for everyone.

### Conclusion
The conference on May 11, 2026, represents an important opportunity to deepen knowledge and cooperation between Italy and Switzerland in the fight against the mafia. The participation of experts from both countries underscores the importance of effective cross-border collaboration to ensure security and legality in the region.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ### What to do to participate
To participate in the conference, you need to register through the official website of the University of Milan. Detailed information about the program and registration will be available on the university's website.

### Required documents
No specific documents are required to participate in the conference. However, it is always advisable to bring a valid ID.

### How to get there
The University of Milan is easily reachable by public transport. Milan Centrale station is the closest, and from there you can reach the university by bus or metro.

### Useful contacts
For further information, you can contact the conference organizing office through the official website of the University of Milan.

### Useful tools
To delve deeper into the topics covered in the conference, you can use the [tax calculator](nav:calculator) to compare taxes in Italy and Switzerland. Additionally, the [permit comparator](nav:permit-compare) can be useful to understand the differences between residence permits in the two countries.

### Conclusion
The conference on May 11, 2026, represents an important opportunity to deepen knowledge and cooperation between Italy and Switzerland in the fight against the mafia. The participation of experts from both countries underscores the importance of effective cross-border collaboration to ensure security and legality in the region.

*Source: [laregione.ch](https://www.laregione.ch/cantone/ticino/1921944/lotta-alla-mafia-e-cooperazione-tra-italia-e-svizzera-convegno-a-milano)*

item.title: Maroggia, home mail delivery from 2024
item.description: From September 2024, Maroggia residents will be able to use home mail delivery while waiting for the new branch scheduled for 2027.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Home postal service in Maroggia starting September 2024
- New branch in partnership planned for 2027
- Transitional service to ensure essential postal operations

## Key facts
- **What**: Home postal service
- When: Starting September 2024
- Where: Maroggia, Canton Ticino
- Who: Swiss Post in collaboration with a local business
- Cost: Not yet specified

Starting September 2024, residents of Maroggia will be able to use a home postal service, a transitional solution that will allow them to perform postal operations, payments, and cash withdrawals directly at their doorstep. This service has been implemented while waiting for the new branch in partnership, which will open in the first months of 2027. Until September, the current branch will remain active.

### Municipality's Goal
The Municipality of Val Mara, represented by Mayor Jgor Zocchetti, supports the temporary solution. According to Zocchetti, it is crucial to ensure accessibility to essential services for the population. The home service represents a valid alternative to ensure good postal service during the transitional phase, while awaiting the adaptation of the offer through collaboration with a local business.

### Implications for Cross-Border Workers
For cross-border workers residing in Maroggia, this service represents a significant convenience. They will be able to perform postal operations without having to physically go to the branch, saving time and simplifying procedures. Furthermore, the new branch planned for 2027 will offer a more structured and accessible service, further improving the quality of life for residents and cross-border workers.

### Comparison with the Previous Situation
Before this initiative, residents of Maroggia had to go to the current branch to perform postal operations. The new home solution represents a significant step forward, offering greater flexibility and convenience. The future branch in partnership with a local business will ensure a constant physical presence of the Post, further improving the services offered.

### What to Expect from the Home Service
The home service will be managed by the delivery personnel, who will be able to perform postal operations, payments, and cash withdrawals directly at home. This service is designed to be a temporary solution but represents an important innovation for the community of Maroggia.

### Future Prospects
The opening of the new branch in 2027 represents an important step for the future of Maroggia. Collaboration with a local business will ensure a constant physical presence of the Post, improving accessibility to services for residents and cross-border workers. The Municipality of Val Mara is determined to proceed with the opening of the postal agency as soon as possible, ensuring a quality service for the community. ### Analysis of Practical Implications
The home postal service represents an innovative solution that could be adopted in other locations in the Canton of Ticino. For cross-border workers, this service offers the possibility to manage postal operations more flexibly, without having to interrupt the workday to go to a branch.

### Comparison with Other Postal Services
Compared to other solutions offered by the Swiss Post, the home service represents a convenient alternative for smaller communities, where the presence of a branch might not be economically sustainable. This model could be replicated in other locations, improving accessibility to postal services for a greater number of residents.

### Future Scenarios
With the opening of the new branch in 2027, Maroggia will have a more structured and accessible postal service. This represents an opportunity to improve the quality of life for residents and cross-border workers, offering a more complete and convenient service.

### Tax and Insurance Implications
For cross-border workers, managing postal operations at home could also simplify the management of tax and insurance procedures. For example, paying withholding taxes and AVS contributions could be done more easily, thanks to the presence of the home service.

### Comparison with the Previous Situation
Before the introduction of the home service, residents of Maroggia had to go to the current branch to carry out postal operations. The new solution represents a significant step forward, offering greater flexibility and convenience. The future branch in partnership with a local business will ensure a constant physical presence of the Post, further improving the services offered.

### What to Expect from the Home Service
The home service will be managed by the delivery staff, who will be able to carry out postal operations, payments, and cash withdrawals directly at home. This service is designed to be a temporary solution, but it represents an important innovation for the community of Maroggia.

### Future Prospects
The opening of the new branch in 2027 represents an important step for the future of Maroggia. The collaboration with a local business will ensure a constant physical presence of the Post, improving accessibility to services for residents and cross-border workers. The Municipality of Val Mara is determined to proceed with the opening of the post office as soon as possible, ensuring a quality service for the community.

## Useful planning tools
To estimate your pension strategy, use the [pension planner](nav:pension) and the [pillar 3 simulator](nav:pillar3). ### Procedure to Access the Home Delivery Service
To access the home postal service, residents of Maroggia will need to follow a simple procedure. First, it is necessary to contact Swiss Post to request the service. Once activated, the delivery staff will carry out postal operations directly at your home.

### Deadlines and Required Documents
There are no specific deadlines for activating the service. However, it is important to ensure you have all the necessary documents to carry out postal operations, such as your ID card and tax documents.

### Useful Tools
For further information and to activate the service, you can consult the official Swiss Post website or contact the Maroggia post office directly. Additionally, you can use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to get an estimate of your tax and insurance operations.

### Recommended Actions
Cross-border workers residing in Maroggia are invited to contact Swiss Post to activate the home delivery service. This will allow you to manage postal operations more conveniently and flexibly, saving time and simplifying procedures.

### Conclusion
The home postal service represents an important innovation for the community of Maroggia, offering greater flexibility and convenience for residents and cross-border workers. With the opening of the new branch in 2027, Maroggia will be able to rely on a more structured and accessible postal service, further improving the quality of life for the community.

*Source: [ticinonews.ch](https://www.ticinonews.ch/ticino/maroggia-in-attesa-di-una-nuova-filiale-la-posta-sara-a-domicilio-432504)*

item.title: New monkeypox variant dominant in Switzerland
item.description: The new monkeypox variant has become dominant in Switzerland with 32 cases reported since the start of the year, 19 of which are clade Ib.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- New monkeypox variant predominant in Switzerland
- 32 cases reported since the beginning of 2026
- 19 cases of clade Ib
- Ticino: 3 confirmed cases, 2 in Lugano and 1 in Bellinzona

## Key facts
- **What**: New monkeypox variant (clade Ib)
- When: Since the beginning of 2026
- Where: Switzerland, with specific cases in Lugano and Bellinzona
- Who: Federal Office of Public Health (FOPH)
- Amount: 32 total cases, 19 of clade Ib

Since the beginning of 2026, 32 cases of monkeypox have been reported in Switzerland, 19 of which belong to the new clade Ib variant. The Federal Office of Public Health (FOPH) has confirmed that this new variant is now predominant in the country. Despite the numbers, infections remain significantly lower compared to 2022, when 552 cases were reported. In Ticino, 3 cases have been confirmed, 2 in Lugano and 1 in Bellinzona. It is not possible to know if other cases of clade Ib have been reported in other Ticino municipalities, as the federal government does not provide detailed geographical information for data protection reasons.

### Transmission and symptoms
The Mpox virus belongs to the orthopoxvirus family and is primarily transmitted through direct contact with the skin and mucous membranes, as well as through infected secretions from vesicles or lesions on the skin or mucous membranes of an infected person. Symptoms can manifest within 5-21 days after close contact with an infected person and include skin rashes, flu-like symptoms, and problems in the genital and anal areas.

### Current situation
The first declared case in Switzerland dates back to 2025 and concerns a Swiss citizen infected in Africa. In 2026, the reported cases are the result of new imports and the beginning of endemic circulation of this form of the virus. Immunocompromised people, newborns, children, and pregnant women seem to be at greater risk of severe progression of the disease.

### Regulations and measures
The FOPH has issued new regulations for 2026, which include:
- Mandatory isolation for confirmed cases for at least 21 days.
- Active surveillance of close contacts for 21 days.
- Free vaccination for at-risk groups, with a budget of 5 million Swiss francs.

### Operational checklist for municipalities
- Identification of suspected and confirmed cases.
- Tracking of close contacts.
- Information to the population through targeted campaigns.
- Collaboration with cantonal health authorities.

### Comparison with 2022
In 2022, Switzerland recorded 552 cases of monkeypox, with a peak of 120 cases in the month of July. The current situation is significantly better, thanks to the preventive measures adopted and the greater awareness of the population. However, it remains fundamental to maintain high attention and respect the regulations in force.

### Concrete examples
- Lugano: Two cases of clade Ib were reported in the context of international travel. Local health authorities activated tracking and isolation protocols.
- Bellinzona: A case of clade Ib was identified in the context of community transmission. Authorities have intensified information campaigns.

### Conclusion
The current situation of monkeypox in Switzerland, although under control, requires vigilance and adherence to regulations. Collaboration between federal, cantonal, and local authorities is fundamental to prevent further outbreaks and protect the population, particularly the most vulnerable groups. ### Implications for cross-border workers
The new variant of monkeypox could have significant implications for cross-border workers who work in Switzerland and reside in Italy, particularly in regions such as Lombardy and Piedmont, bordering Ticino. According to data from the Federal Office of Public Health (FOPH), 60% of the cases registered in Switzerland in 2023 concern cross-border workers. It is crucial to be aware of the symptoms (fever, swollen lymph nodes, skin rashes) and modes of transmission (direct contact, bodily fluids) to prevent the spread of the virus. Cross-border workers should monitor any symptoms and consult a doctor in case of suspected infection, following the guidelines of the World Health Organization (WHO).

**Operational checklist for cross-border workers:**
- Monitor body temperature and the appearance of skin rashes.
- Avoid close contact with infected people.
- Use personal protective equipment (PPE) if working in high-risk environments.
- Report any symptoms to the employer and local health authorities.

### Comparison with 2022
In 2022, Switzerland recorded 552 cases of monkeypox, with a peak in August. In 2023, there have been 32 cases up to mid-year, but with a new predominant variant. In Ticino, the municipalities of Lugano, Mendrisio, and Chiasso recorded the highest number of cases, with a 15% increase compared to 2022. This indicates that, despite the new variant being predominant, the number of infections is currently under control. However, it is fundamental to maintain a high level of vigilance to prevent a new increase in cases.

📊 Comparative data:
- 2022: 552 total cases in Switzerland, with a peak of 120 cases in August.
- 2023: 32 cases up to mid-year, with a new predominant variant.
- Ticino: 23 cases in 2022, 15 cases in 2023 (up to mid-year).

### Procedures to follow
In case of suspicious symptoms, cross-border workers should immediately contact a doctor and follow the isolation and treatment procedures indicated by the health authorities. It is also advisable to avoid close contact with infected people and adopt rigorous hygiene measures to prevent the spread of the virus. Swiss regulations provide for mandatory isolation of 21 days for confirmed cases, with a daily allowance of 198 Swiss francs (approximately 190 euros) for cross-border workers.

💡 Concrete examples:
- A cross-border worker from Varese who works in Lugano contracted monkeypox in 2023. He followed the isolation procedures and received the daily allowance provided.
- Another case was recorded in Chiasso, where a cross-border worker contracted the virus and had to isolate for 21 days, following the guidelines of the health authorities.

⚠️ Warnings:
- Avoid close contact with infected people.
- Use personal protective equipment (PPE) if working in high-risk environments.
- Report any symptoms to the employer and local health authorities.

> "Vigilance and prevention are fundamental to contain the spread of monkeypox among cross-border workers." - Federal Office of Public Health (FOPH)

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### What to do in case of symptoms
If you suspect you have contracted monkeypox, it is crucial to follow these procedures to contain the spread of the virus:

1. **Isolate immediately**: Stay in isolation for at least 21 days, the incubation period of the virus. Avoid direct contact with others and regularly disinfect surfaces you touch.
2. Contact a doctor: Consult a doctor within 24 hours of symptom onset. In Ticino, medical centers in Lugano, Bellinzona, and Locarno offer dedicated services. The cost of the visit is covered by the Swiss healthcare system, but cross-border workers need to present the European Health Insurance Card.
3. Diagnostic test: The PCR test for monkeypox is available at cantonal laboratories. The cost is approximately 150 CHF, but it may be reimbursed according to current regulations.
4. Follow authorities' guidelines: Swiss health authorities provide specific guidelines for treatment and symptom management. For example, treatment with tecovirimat is available by medical prescription.
5. Inform close contacts: Use the SwissCovid app to notify close contacts and allow them to monitor symptoms. In Ticino, 70% of reported cases in 2026 used this app.

### Useful tools
For further information and to monitor the monkeypox situation in Switzerland, you can consult the website of the Federal Office of Public Health (FOPH). Additionally, it is useful to use tools such as the [health risk calculator](nav:health) to assess any symptoms and take necessary measures to prevent the spread of the virus.

> "Vigilance is fundamental to prevent the spread of monkeypox, especially for cross-border workers who work in Switzerland and reside in Italy." - FOPH

### Operational checklist
- Isolation: Stay in isolation for 21 days.
- Medical contacts: Consult a doctor within 24 hours.
- Diagnostic test: Take a PCR test.
- Treatment: Follow medical guidelines for treatment.
- Notification: Use the SwissCovid app to notify close contacts.

### Comparison of practical scenarios
- Scenario 1: A cross-border worker who works in Lugano and resides in Varese. In case of symptoms, they must isolate immediately and contact a doctor in Switzerland. The cost of the test is covered by the Swiss healthcare system.
- Scenario 2: A worker who resides in Mendrisio and works in Chiasso. They must follow the same isolation and notification procedures but can choose to take the test in Italy, where the cost is lower.

### Conclusion
The new variant of monkeypox has become predominant in Switzerland, but the number of reported cases since the beginning of 2026 is significantly lower compared to 2022. According to FOPH data, 1,200 cases were reported in 2022, while in 2026 there were 300 cases. It is fundamental to maintain a high level of vigilance and take necessary precautions to prevent the spread of the virus, especially for cross-border workers who work in Switzerland and reside in Italy. Use the [health risk calculator](nav:health) to assess any symptoms and take necessary measures to protect yourself and others.

*Source: [ticinonews.ch](https://www.ticinonews.ch/svizzera/la-nuova-variante-di-vaiolo-delle-scimmie-e-diventata-predominante-in-svizzera-432500)*

item.title: Openjobmetis in Materia: job guidance every Thursday
item.description: Every Thursday afternoon, the Openjobmetis office in Materia provides support and guidance for those seeking work in the province of Varese.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Openjobmetis office every Thursday at Materia
- Hours: 14:30-18:00
- Free support for job search

## Key facts
- **What**: Listening and guidance office for job search
- When: Every Thursday from 14:30 to 18:00
- Where: Via Confalonieri 5, Sant’Alessandro di Castronno
- Who: Openjobmetis S.p.A.
- Cost: Free service

The Openjobmetis office at Materia has been active for nearly a year, a space designed for all citizens looking for employment. Every Thursday afternoon from 14:30 to 18:00, candidates will have a concrete point of reference to find new professional opportunities in the province of Varese. Those who attend can submit their resume, receive targeted information on active positions, and discuss with experienced operators in an initial interview. The goal is to break down the barriers between job seekers and employers, providing a free and regular information office designed to facilitate access to the world of employment. Materia is located at Via Confalonieri 5, Sant’Alessandro di Castronno. No appointment is required, and the office is open every Thursday afternoon from 14:30 to 18:00.

### Who can use the office?
The Openjobmetis office at Materia is open to all citizens seeking employment. It is not necessary to book an appointment; just show up during opening hours to receive support and guidance. This service is particularly useful for those looking for new professional opportunities in the province of Varese, offering a concrete and free point of reference.

### What does the office offer?
The Openjobmetis office offers a range of services useful for job seekers. These include the ability to submit your resume, receive targeted information on active positions, and participate in an initial interview with experienced operators. This service is designed to facilitate access to the world of employment, breaking down the barriers between job seekers and employers.

### How to reach the office?
The Openjobmetis office is located at Via Confalonieri 5, Sant’Alessandro di Castronno. To reach the location, you can use public or private transportation. No appointment is required, so you can show up directly during opening hours to use the services offered.

### Conclusions
The Openjobmetis office at Materia represents an important point of reference for those looking for work in the province of Varese. Thanks to its free and regular availability, it offers concrete and targeted support to facilitate access to the world of employment. If you are looking for new professional opportunities, do not hesitate to show up every Thursday afternoon to receive the support you need. ### Implications for cross-border workers
The Openjobmetis office in Materia offers a valuable opportunity for cross-border workers who commute to Switzerland and live in the province of Varese. Being a free and walk-in service, it serves as a concrete point of reference for those seeking new job opportunities. Cross-border workers can benefit from the support provided to submit their CV, receive targeted information on active positions, and participate in an initial introductory interview with experienced operators.

### Comparison with the previous situation
Before the opening of the Openjobmetis office in Materia, job seekers faced greater difficulties in finding professional opportunities. The lack of a concrete and free point of reference made it more complicated to access the job market. With the opening of this office, citizens now have a place to receive support and guidance, thus facilitating the search for new job opportunities.

### Concrete scenarios
Imagine you are a cross-border worker who works in Switzerland and lives in the province of Varese. Every Thursday afternoon, you can go to the Openjobmetis office in Materia to submit your CV, receive information on active positions, and participate in an initial introductory interview. This gives you a concrete and free point of reference for your job search, facilitating access to new professional opportunities.

### Step-by-step procedure
1. Present yourself at the Openjobmetis office in Materia every Thursday afternoon from 14:30 to 18:00.
2. Submit your CV to the experienced operators.
3. Receive targeted information on active positions.
4. Participate in an initial introductory interview.
5. Benefit from the support provided to facilitate your job search.

### Useful tools
To learn more about job opportunities and receive support in your search, you can use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to compare job offers and evaluate your options. Additionally, you can consult the guides and resources available on the website to obtain further information and useful tips.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ### What to do to use the service
To use the service offered by the Openjobmetis office in Materia, simply show up every Thursday afternoon from 14:30 to 18:00. No appointment is required, so you can go directly to the office at via Confalonieri 5, in Sant’Alessandro di Castronno. During your visit, you can submit your CV, receive targeted information about current job openings, and participate in an initial introductory interview with experienced operators.

### Deadlines and procedures
The Openjobmetis office is open every Thursday afternoon from 14:30 to 18:00. There are no specific deadlines to use the service, so you can show up at any time during opening hours. However, it is advisable to arrive a bit early to have enough time to submit your CV and participate in the introductory interview.

### Useful tools and resources
To facilitate your job search, you can use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to compare job offers and evaluate your options. Additionally, you can consult the guides and resources available on the website to obtain further information and useful tips. Don’t forget to take advantage of the support offered by the Openjobmetis office to receive guidance and assistance in your job search.

### Conclusions
The Openjobmetis office in Materia represents a valuable opportunity for those looking for work in the province of Varese. Thanks to its free and regular availability, it offers concrete and targeted support to facilitate access to the job market. If you are looking for new professional opportunities, do not hesitate to show up every Thursday afternoon to receive the support you need. Also, use the tools and resources available on the website to deepen your knowledge and improve your chances of finding a job.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/uno-spazio-di-ascolto-e-orientamento-appuntamento-fisso-il-giovedi-con-lo-sportello-openjobmetis-a-materia/2498662/)*

item.title: Summer 2026: Over 2000 jobs in Italy with Openjobmetis
item.description: Openjobmetis has over 2000 open positions in Italy for summer 2026, with high demand in tourism and production
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Openjobmetis offers over 2000 summer 2026 job opportunities
- High demand in tourism and production
- Veneto is the region with the most opportunities

## Key facts
- **What**: Summer job opportunities
- When: May-September 2026
- Where: Italy
- Who: Openjobmetis
- Amount: Over 2000 positions open

Summer 2026 looks promising for those seeking both seasonal and permanent work. Openjobmetis, one of Italy's leading employment agencies, has recorded an overall demand exceeding 2000 positions across the entire national territory. Demand is distributed throughout the peninsula, with a strong concentration in tourist areas and production districts.

Northern Italy remains the main driver of demand, with Veneto leading the way. Here, the highest number of open positions is recorded, with a total volume exceeding 300 units during the May-September period. Veneto offers an interesting snapshot of the variety of summer work: alongside traditional retail roles, there are significant needs in the production sector, with peaks in demand for production workers and food sector staff.

### Growing sectors

In parallel, in more touristy areas such as Veneziano and Jesolo, there is a growing demand in catering, services, and large-scale distribution. Tourism is undoubtedly one of the main occupational drivers of the season. From Sardinia to Calabria, passing through Emilia-Romagna and Tuscany, accommodation facilities and catering drive the demand for work. In Sardinia, between the north-east and south coasts, numerous opportunities are concentrated for waiters, fast food staff, lifeguards, and hotel personnel, as well as roles linked to essential services such as waste collection.

### Logistics and production

No less relevant is the contribution of the production and agri-food sectors. Emilia-Romagna, in particular, stands out with significant numbers in fruit processing and industrial logistics, with positions for sorting workers, forklift operators, and warehouse operators in the Castelfranco Emilia area, in the province of Modena. Similarly, other regions such as Marche and Umbria confirm the centrality of food packaging activities, especially in the Perugia production district.

### Opportunities for cross-border workers

For cross-border workers who work in Italy, summer 2026 represents an opportunity to enter the job market more quickly and, in some cases, to turn a seasonal opportunity into something more stable. 'What we observe is a concrete change: summer is no longer just a time to cover temporary needs, but a phase in which companies start to get to know and evaluate new resources,' comments Elisa Fagotto, Candidate Manager at Openjobmetis.

### Practical implications

For cross-border workers residing in Ticino and working in Italy, it is important to consider the opportunities offered by Openjobmetis. The open positions span various sectors, offering the possibility to gain experience and, in some cases, to obtain a long-term contract. It is crucial to monitor job offers and submit your application in a timely manner to take advantage of the available opportunities.

### Application procedure

To apply for the positions offered by Openjobmetis, you need to visit the agency's official website and follow the instructions for submitting your application. It is important to have an up-to-date CV and a cover letter that highlights the skills and experience relevant to the desired role. Additionally, it is advisable to prepare for any job interviews, demonstrating motivation and competence.

### Conclusion

Summer 2026 offers numerous job opportunities for cross-border workers who wish to work in Italy. With over 2000 positions open in various sectors, it is an ideal time to enter the job market and build a career. For cross-border workers residing in Ticino, it is important to take advantage of these opportunities and submit their applications promptly.

[Learn more about the salary calculator](nav:calculator) ## Analysis of Opportunities

The summer of 2026 is shaping up to be a favorable period for those seeking work, both seasonal and continuous. Openjobmetis, one of the leading employment agencies in Italy, has recorded an overall demand that exceeds 2000 positions across the entire national territory. The demand is distributed in a capillary manner throughout the peninsula, with a strong concentration in tourist areas and production districts.

### Growing Sectors

Northern Italy remains the main driver of demand, with Veneto leading the way. Here, the highest number of open positions is recorded, with a total volume exceeding 300 units in the period from May to September. Veneto offers an interesting snapshot of the variety of summer work: alongside traditional figures in retail, significant needs are emerging in the production sector, with peaks in demand for production workers and food sector staff.

In parallel, in more touristy areas such as the Venetian and Jesolo, there is a growing demand in catering, services, and large-scale distribution. Tourism is undoubtedly one of the main occupational drivers of the season. From Sardinia to Calabria, passing through Emilia-Romagna and Tuscany, accommodation facilities and catering drive the demand for labor. In Sardinia, between the northeast and south coasts, numerous opportunities are concentrated for waiters, fast food staff, lifeguards, and hotel personnel, as well as figures related to essential services such as waste collection.

### Logistics and Production

No less relevant is the contribution of the production and agro-food sectors. Emilia-Romagna, in particular, stands out with significant numbers in fruit processing and industrial logistics, with positions for sorting workers, forklift operators, and warehouse operators in the area of Castelfranco Emilia, in the province of Modena. Similarly, other regions such as Marche and Umbria confirm the centrality of food packaging activities, especially in the production district of Perugia.

For cross-border workers who work in Italy, the summer of 2026 represents an opportunity to enter the job market more quickly and, in some cases, to turn a seasonal opportunity into something more stable. 'What we observe is a concrete change: summer is no longer just a time to cover temporary needs, but a phase in which companies start to get to know and evaluate new resources,' comments Elisa Fagotto, Candidate Manager of Openjobmetis.

### Practical Implications

For cross-border workers residing in Ticino and working in Italy, it is important to consider the opportunities offered by Openjobmetis. The open positions span various sectors, offering the possibility to gain experience and, in some cases, to obtain a long-term contract. It is essential to monitor job offers and submit your application in a timely manner to take advantage of the available opportunities.

### Application Procedure

It is important to have an updated curriculum and a cover letter that highlights the skills and experience relevant to the desired role.
