item.title: Cross-border workers: Unemployment paid by the country where they worked
item.description: EU reform approved: significant changes for cross-border workers in Switzerland. Here's what changes and what to do
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- EU reform approved for the coordination of social security systems
- Swiss cross-border workers could benefit from the country's social benefits where they work
- Switzerland is not obliged to automatically adapt

## Key facts
- **What**: Reform of the coordination of EU social security systems
- When: April 29, 2026
- Where: Brussels
- Who: EU member states, Switzerland
- Vote: 21 in favor, 4 against, 2 abstentions

The reform of the coordination of the European Union's social security systems, approved on Wednesday with 21 votes in favor, 4 against and 2 abstentions, could have a significant impact on cross-border workers in Switzerland. The amendment, discussed for a decade, introduces the principle of the validity of the workplace for social benefits, particularly those intended for the unemployed. This means that a cross-border worker who becomes unemployed could benefit from the coverage of the country where they have worked and paid contributions, rather than the state where they reside.

Currently, an unemployed cross-border worker falls under the system of the state where they reside and benefits from the benefits provided by the latter. With the reform, however, the same worker would benefit from the coverage of the country where they worked. Twenty-two uninterrupted weeks of contributions will entitle them to 6 months of unemployment benefits, if the internal requirements of the last country of work are met.

The Cypriot presidency announced the agreement, which brings 'long-awaited clarity,' as explained by the country's labor minister, Marinos Moushouttas. The new rules will make it easier for people living or working in another European state to understand and access the benefits and entitlements they are entitled to. This could lead to greater freedom of movement, stronger labor markets, and a more competitive and socially just Europe for all.

### Impact on Switzerland
Switzerland is not obliged to automatically adapt to this reform. The Confederation could decide not to implement the novelty, but in case of refusal, a new front with Brussels could open, which could adopt retaliatory measures on other dossiers. The final decision will be up to the Swiss Parliament. The reform of the coordination of the EU's social security systems represents a significant change for cross-border workers employed in Switzerland. Currently, unemployed cross-border workers benefit from the provisions of the state in which they reside. With the new reform, however, they could benefit from the provisions of the country in which they have worked and paid contributions.

### What changes for cross-border workers

Cross-border workers employed in Switzerland who become unemployed could benefit from 6 months of unemployment benefits, provided they have paid contributions for at least 22 uninterrupted weeks and meet the internal requirements of the last country of employment. This change could have a significant impact on the lives of cross-border workers, who could have access to more favorable benefits in the country where they have worked.

### Comparison with the current situation

Currently, unemployed cross-border workers benefit from the provisions of the state in which they reside. With the new reform, however, they could benefit from the provisions of the country in which they have worked. This could lead to greater equity and a strengthening of labor markets.

### Possible scenarios

If Switzerland decided not to comply with the reform, it could open a new front with Brussels. The European Commission could adopt retaliatory measures on other dossiers, such as trade relations or the free movement of persons. On the other hand, if Switzerland decided to comply, cross-border workers could benefit from more favorable benefits and greater freedom of movement.

### Practical implications

Cross-border workers employed in Switzerland should closely monitor the evolution of the situation and prepare for any changes in unemployment benefits. It is important that cross-border workers are aware of their rights and the possibilities of accessing benefits both in the country of residence and in the country of work.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). The reform of the EU's coordination of social security systems represents an opportunity for cross-border workers employed in Switzerland to benefit from more favorable benefits. However, it is important that cross-border workers are aware of their rights and the possibilities of accessing benefits in both their country of residence and their country of work.

### What to do now
Cross-border workers employed in Switzerland should closely monitor the evolution of the situation and prepare for any changes in unemployment benefits. It is important that cross-border workers are aware of their rights and the possibilities of accessing benefits in both their country of residence and their country of work.

### Procedure to access benefits
If the reform is approved and Switzerland decides to comply, cross-border workers will need to follow a specific procedure to access unemployment benefits in the country where they have worked. It is important that cross-border workers inform themselves at the competent offices and follow the instructions provided.

### Useful tools
To help cross-border workers better understand their rights and the possibilities of accessing benefits, several online tools are available. For example, the [unemployment benefits calculator](nav:calculator) can help cross-border workers estimate the benefits they are entitled to. Additionally, the [benefits comparator](nav:unemployment) can help cross-border workers compare the benefits offered in different countries.

### Conclusion
The reform of the EU's coordination of social security systems represents an opportunity for cross-border workers employed in Switzerland to benefit from more favorable benefits. However, it is important that cross-border workers are aware of their rights and the possibilities of accessing benefits in both their country of residence and their country of work. Closely monitoring the evolution of the situation and preparing for any changes in unemployment benefits is crucial to ensure a smooth and advantageous transition.

*Source: [rsi.ch](https://www.rsi.ch/s/3704307)*

item.title: Third anti-food waste fridge inaugurated in Tenero
item.description: On April 28, 2026, a third anti-waste public fridge was inaugurated in Tenero, Ticino, promoted by the Madame Frigo association.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Third anti-waste fridge inaugurated in Tenero
- Initiative promoted by Madame Frigo
- Goal: combat food waste

## Key facts
- **What**: Inauguration of a public anti-waste fridge
- When: April 28, 2026
- Where: Tenero, Ticino
- Who: Madame Frigo Association
- Amount: Third fridge in Ticino

On April 28, 2026, the third public anti-food waste fridge promoted by the Madame Frigo association was inaugurated in Tenero, Ticino. This new fridge joins those already present in Locarno and Pregassona, bringing the total number of active stations in the canton to three. The initiative, born with the aim of combating food waste, allows those who have too much food to leave it and those who need it to take it. The fridge in Tenero was inaugurated on Via Verbano and represents another step towards creating a culture of sharing and sustainability.

The Madame Frigo project, coordinated by the Lucerne association of the same name, is already widely spread in inland Switzerland and is now expanding into Ticino. In Locarno, the public fridge has been placed outside the hangar space of the Area cooperative, along with other local associations such as Okkio and Basta poco. Alissa Hernandez Pita, one of the promoters of the project in Locarno, explained that the yellow fridge is used daily, with vegetables and bread being the most frequently deposited and taken food items.

### How the project works

The operation of the public fridge is simple: those who have excess food can leave it in the fridge, while those who need it can take it. However, there are some rules to follow. In Tenero, for example, a QR code will allow joining a dedicated WhatsApp group, facilitating communication among participants. Despite the presence of multiple fridges, there is no competition between the different stations. Alissa Hernandez Pita emphasized that the more fridges there are, the better, as the main goal is to reduce food waste.

### The challenge of filling the fridges

One of the main challenges of the project is managing to fill the fridges with food. Hernandez Pita explained that the difficulty is not so much in disposing of the merchandise that arrives, but in finding people or companies willing to donate their food. It is necessary to further raise awareness among the population and create a culture of sharing that, at the moment, is not yet as rooted in Ticino as in other Swiss cantons.

### The goal of expanding the project

Madame Frigo is present in Switzerland with almost 190 stations and the goal is to further grow the project also south of the Alps. The initiative represents an important step towards reducing food waste and promoting a more sustainable society. With the inauguration of the third fridge in Tenero, the project continues to expand, involving more and more people and communities in Ticino. The inauguration of the third anti-food waste public fridge in Tenero represents an important opportunity for cross-border workers who work in Ticino. This project not only promotes sustainability but also offers concrete support to those who might be facing economic difficulties. Cross-border workers, who often have to manage high costs, can benefit from this initiative to access fresh and quality food without incurring additional expenses.

### Implications for Cross-Border Workers

For cross-border workers in Ticino, the Madame Frigo project is a valuable resource. Many of them might find themselves in situations where the food budget is limited, and access to a public fridge can make a difference. Furthermore, the initiative promotes a culture of sharing and solidarity, values that can be particularly appreciated by those living and working in a cross-border context.

### Comparisons with the Previous Situation

Before the introduction of public fridges, cross-border workers facing economic difficulties had to rely on other forms of assistance, such as food banks or private donations. With the arrival of Madame Frigo, they now have a more immediate and accessible solution. This change represents a significant step forward towards greater inclusion and support for the cross-border worker community.

### Concrete Scenarios

Imagine a cross-border worker who works in Locarno and, due to unforeseen economic circumstances, finds themselves in difficulty at the end of the month. Thanks to the public fridge, they can access fresh and quality food without incurring additional expenses. At the same time, another cross-border worker who has excess food can choose to donate it, thus helping to reduce food waste and assisting those in need. This reciprocal exchange creates a virtuous circle that benefits the entire community.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). For cross-border workers interested in participating in the Madame Frigo project, there are some procedures to follow. First, it is necessary to find the nearest fridge, such as those in Tenero, Locarno, or Pregassona. Once identified, you can start donating or collecting food following the established rules. In Tenero, for example, a QR code allows access to a dedicated WhatsApp group, facilitating communication among participants.

### Step-by-step procedure

1. **Find the nearest fridge**: Identify the nearest public fridge to you, such as those in Tenero, Locarno, or Pregassona.
2. Follow the rules: Make sure to follow the rules established for donating and collecting food.
3. Join the WhatsApp group: In Tenero, use the QR code to join the dedicated group and stay updated on the latest news.
4. Donation and collection: Donate or collect food based on your needs, thus contributing to reducing food waste.

### Deadlines and useful tools

There are no specific deadlines to participate in the Madame Frigo project, but it is important to stay updated on the news and local initiatives. Use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to plan your budget and discover how to save even more. Additionally, consult the guides and tools available on the site to learn more about the opportunities offered by the project.

### Final CTA

Find out more about the Madame Frigo project and how to participate. Use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to plan your budget and discover how to save even more. Additionally, consult the guides and tools available on the site to learn more about the opportunities offered by the project.

*Source: [rsi.ch](https://www.rsi.ch/s/3704490)*

item.title: Mendrisio closes 2025 with fourth positive balance
item.description: Mendrisio's municipal council announces a fourth consecutive positive financial result, despite geopolitical uncertainties.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Mendrisio records its fourth consecutive positive balance in 2025
- Municipal finances remain 'comfortable'
- Awaiting the effects of reforms and the geopolitical context

## Key facts
- **What**: 2025 Municipal Budget
- When: 2025
- Where: Mendrisio
- Who: Mendrisio Municipality
- Amount: Fourth consecutive positive result

Mendrisio sums up 2025 and achieves its fourth consecutive positive result. Municipal finances remain 'comfortable', despite uncertainties related to reforms and the geopolitical landscape. The Municipality of Mendrisio announced today the financial results for the year 2025, highlighting fiscal stability that has rewarded the city. This positive result is part of a series of four consecutive years of surplus budgets, a sign of prudent and careful financial management.

The mayor of Mendrisio, Samuele Cavadini, commented on the results emphasizing the importance of maintaining a solid financial balance in a globally uncertain economic context. 'We are satisfied with the results achieved, but we remain vigilant in the face of future challenges,' declared Cavadini. Uncertainties related to tax reforms and the geopolitical context require careful management of resources, especially in an area like Ticino, where cross-border work plays a crucial role.

### Implications for cross-border workers

Cross-border workers who work in Mendrisio and neighboring municipalities can benefit from greater fiscal stability, which translates into efficient public services and a more secure economic environment. However, geopolitical uncertainties and tax reforms could impact withholding taxes and refunds, fundamental elements for cross-border workers. It is important to monitor regulatory developments to adapt their financial strategies.

### Comparison with previous years

Over the past four years, Mendrisio has demonstrated financial management capabilities that have allowed it to maintain a surplus budget. This positive trend is a sign of stability for the local community and for cross-border workers who choose to work in this region. However, the global economic context and tax reforms could influence this trend in the coming years.

### What to expect for the future

It is advisable to regularly consult the official communications of the Municipality and the competent authorities to stay updated on the latest news. ## Analysis of the implications for cross-border workers

However, it is important to consider the implications of tax reforms and the geopolitical context on their net income. Withholding taxes and refunds are crucial elements for cross-border workers, and any changes to these aspects could have a significant impact.

### Comparison with the previous situation

In recent years, Mendrisio has maintained a positive balance, which has allowed it to offer efficient public services and a stable economic environment. This positive trend is an advantage for cross-border workers, who can count on greater financial security. However, uncertainties related to tax reforms and the geopolitical context could influence this stability.

### What to do to prepare

item.title: Italian mayors oppose Lake Maggiore rise
item.description: The mayors of Verbania, Baveno, and Cannobio criticize the decision to raise Lake Maggiore's maximum level to 1.40 meters.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Mayors of Verbania, Baveno, and Cannobio oppose the rise of Lake Maggiore
- Maximum level raised to 1.40 meters above zero hydrometric
- Criticism for damage to tourism and local economy

## Key facts
- **What**: Raising the maximum level of Lake Maggiore
- When: Recent decision, not yet specified
- Where: Lake Maggiore, border between Italy and Switzerland
- Who: Mayors of Verbania, Baveno, and Cannobio
- Amount: 1.40 meters above zero hydrometric

The mayors of Verbania, Giandomenico Albertella, Baveno, Alessandro Monti, and Cannobio, Gianmaria Minazzi, have expressed strong opposition to the decision to raise the maximum level of Lake Maggiore to 1.40 meters above the zero hydrometric level of Sesto Calende. This choice represents an increase of 15 centimeters compared to the previous threshold. The mayors define the decision as 'wrong and not shared with the territory,' arguing that 'one hundred and forty centimeters above zero hydrometric is a wrong choice' compared to the historical threshold of 100 centimeters managed for decades. According to the mayors, this increase will cause significant damage to tourism and the local economy, reducing the beaches, one of the main attractions of the lake.

### Criticism of communication methods
The mayors also protest the way the decision was communicated. 'A process conducted predominantly at a technical level by the Po River Basin Authority, with the support of the irrigation needs of the Po Valley, without adequate protection of the interests of the riverside territories and without the effective involvement of the local administrations most directly affected,' they state. According to the mayors, the Piedmont Region has not exercised its role of representation with sufficient firmness, effectively excluding them from the decision-making venues. The mayors have announced that they will continue to oppose the decision through all available legal and political means.

### Implications for cross-border workers
For cross-border workers living along the shores of Lake Maggiore, this decision could have a significant impact. The beaches, often frequented by residents and tourists, could be reduced, influencing local economic activities. Additionally, the rise in the lake's level could affect coastal infrastructure, requiring possible maintenance or adaptation interventions.

### Comparison with the previous situation
Before this decision, the maximum level of Lake Maggiore was managed at 100 centimeters above the zero hydrometric level. The increase to 1.40 meters represents a significant change that could have long-term repercussions on the local ecosystem and economy. The mayors emphasize that the historical threshold had been managed for decades without problems, suggesting that the current decision might not be necessary.

### What if...
If the decision is maintained, the mayors fear that the negative consequences for tourism and the local economy could be irreversible. The beaches, which attract numerous visitors every year, could become less accessible or less attractive, reducing the flow of tourists and, consequently, the economic income for the local communities. Furthermore, the rise in the lake's level could cause damage to coastal infrastructure, increasing maintenance and adaptation costs for local administrations.

### Opposition procedure
The mayors have announced that they will continue to oppose the decision through all available legal and political means. This could include legal appeals, petitions, and awareness campaigns to involve the community and regional and national authorities. The goal is to obtain a review of the decision or, at least, greater involvement of local administrations in future decisions regarding Lake Maggiore.

### Tools and resources
For cross-border workers interested in monitoring the situation or participating in opposition initiatives, it is possible to consult the websites of the municipalities of Verbania, Baveno, and Cannobio for further information and updates. Additionally, it is possible to contact the local administrations directly to express concerns and suggestions.

### Conclusion
The decision to raise the maximum level of Lake Maggiore to 1.40 meters has sparked strong opposition from the mayors of Verbania, Baveno, and Cannobio. The main concerns are the damage to tourism and the local economy, as well as the lack of involvement of local administrations in the decision-making process. The mayors have announced that they will continue to oppose the decision through all available legal and political means, inviting the community to join their cause. ### Analysis of Economic Implications
The rise in the level of Lake Maggiore could have a significant impact on the local economy, particularly on the tourism sector. The beaches, which are one of the main attractions of the lake, could be reduced, decreasing the number of visitors and, consequently, the economic income for local communities. This could have a cascading effect on commercial activities such as restaurants, shops, and rental services, which depend on the flow of tourists for their survival.

### Environmental Impact
In addition to the economic implications, the rise in the level of the lake could have an environmental impact. Coastal areas could suffer from erosion, and local flora and fauna could be affected. Furthermore, the rise in the level of the lake could increase the risk of flooding in some areas, requiring protective and adaptive measures.

### Comparison with Other Regions
To compare the situation of Lake Maggiore with other regions, it is useful to consider how other coastal areas manage the levels of their lakes and rivers. For example, Lake Como has a similar management, with levels regulated to balance the needs of irrigation and protection of coastal areas. However, each lake has unique characteristics that require specific approaches.

### Future Scenarios
If the decision to raise the level of Lake Maggiore is maintained, local communities will have to adapt to the new conditions. This could include the construction of protective barriers, the requalification of beaches, and the promotion of alternative tourist activities. However, these measures would require significant investments and time to be implemented.

### Involvement of Authorities
It is essential that regional and national authorities listen to the concerns of local administrations and actively involve communities in the management of Lake Maggiore. This could include the creation of joint working groups, the realization of impact studies, and the promotion of public participation initiatives.

### Monitoring Tools
To monitor the evolution of the situation, tools such as the [lake level calculator](nav:calculator) and the [economic impact comparator](nav:cost-of-living) can be used. These tools can help better understand the implications of decisions and evaluate the available options.

### Conclusion
The rise in the level of Lake Maggiore represents a complex challenge that requires a balanced and participatory approach. It is essential that authorities, local administrations, and communities work together to find solutions that balance the needs of irrigation, the protection of coastal areas, and economic and environmental sustainability.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ### Concrete actions for cross-border workers
For cross-border workers living along the shores of Lake Maggiore, it is important to be informed and involved in the decision-making process. Here are some concrete actions they can take:

1. **Stay informed**: Consult the websites of the municipalities of Verbania, Baveno, and Cannobio for updates and detailed information on the situation.
2. Participate: Attend public meetings and awareness-raising initiatives organized by local authorities.
3. Express your concerns: Contact the mayors and regional authorities directly to express your concerns and suggestions.
4. Use monitoring tools: Utilize tools such as the [lake level calculator](nav:calculator) and the [economic impact comparator](nav:cost-of-living) to monitor the evolution of the situation.

### Deadlines and procedures
Local authorities are working to define a schedule of meetings and initiatives to engage the community. It is important to stay updated on these deadlines and actively participate in the discussions.

### Tools and resources
For further information and resources, you can consult the following tools:
- [Lake level calculator](nav:calculator)
- [Economic impact comparator](nav:cost-of-living)
- [Guides for public participation](nav:residency)

### Conclusion
The rise in the level of Lake Maggiore represents a significant challenge for local communities and cross-border workers. It is essential that everyone is informed, involved, and ready to actively participate in the decision-making process. By using the available tools and resources, it is possible to monitor the evolution of the situation and contribute to finding sustainable and participatory solutions.

*Source: [ticinonews.ch](https://www.ticinonews.ch/ticino/sindaci-italiani-contro-linnalzamento-del-lago-maggiore-432522)*

item.title: Morning-after pill without consultation, National Council says yes
item.description: The National Council approves a motion to reclassify emergency contraceptives and make them accessible without mandatory consultation.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- The National Council approves motion for pill without consultation
- 113 votes in favor, 73 against and 2 abstentions
- Text now passes to the Council of States

## Key facts
- **What**: Motion to downgrade emergency contraceptives
- When: April 29, 2026
- Where: Bern
- Who: National Council, Corina Gredig (PLF/ZH)
- Vote: 113 in favor, 73 against, 2 abstentions

The National Council has approved a motion proposing to downgrade emergency contraceptives, known as the morning-after pill, to a lower dispensing category. This change would allow access to the pill without the mandatory consultation currently required. The motion, presented by Corina Gredig of the Green Liberal Party of Zurich, passed with 113 votes in favor, 73 against, and 2 abstentions. The text will now pass to the Council of States for further discussion.

Gredig criticized the mandatory consultation, calling it a 'mandatory interview about intimate details' that has a deterrent effect and often accompanies a feeling of shame. 'Every adult and responsible woman should be able to decide freely and without unnecessary barriers whether to take this pill,' Gredig argued. Health Minister Elisabeth Baume-Schneider expressed concerns about patient safety, emphasizing the need for a consultation with a healthcare professional before taking the medication.

### Practical implications
The downgrading of emergency contraceptives could have a significant impact on access to these medications. Currently, pharmacies are required to conduct a consultation before dispensing and to have a specific form filled out. If the motion is approved, the morning-after pill could also be available in drugstores, making it more accessible. However, Minister Baume-Schneider pointed out that drugstores do not have the necessary expertise to provide adequate consultation.

### Comparison with other European countries
Compared to other European countries, access to the morning-after pill in Switzerland is more regulated. Although it has been available without a prescription since 2002, Swiss pharmacies are required to conduct a consultation before dispensing. This requirement has been criticized for its deterrent effect and the feeling of shame it can accompany.

### Next steps
The motion will now pass to the Council of States for further discussion. If approved, it could lead to a significant change in access to emergency contraceptives in Switzerland. However, it is important to note that the health minister has expressed concerns about patient safety and the need for professional consultation before taking the medication. ### Analysis of Practical Implications
The approval of the motion by the National Council could have a significant impact on access to emergency contraceptives in Switzerland. Currently, pharmacies are required to conduct a counseling interview before dispensing the morning-after pill. This requirement has been criticized for its deterrent effect and the feeling of shame that can accompany it.

If the motion is approved, the morning-after pill could also be available in drugstores, making it more accessible. However, Health Minister Elisabeth Baume-Schneider has emphasized that drugstores do not have the necessary skills to provide adequate counseling. Moreover, pharmacies also offer an emergency service on weekends and public holidays, ensuring a continuous supply of these medications.

### Comparison with the Previous Situation
Before 2002, the morning-after pill was only available with a medical prescription in Switzerland. Since 2002, it has become available without a medical prescription, but with the obligation of a counseling interview before dispensing. This change made the pill more accessible, but the mandatory interview has been criticized for its deterrent effect.

### Future Scenarios
If the motion is approved by the Council of States, the morning-after pill could become available without mandatory counseling. This could increase access to the medication, but also raises concerns about patient safety. The health minister has emphasized the need for professional counseling before taking the medication, to ensure that the most appropriate emergency contraceptive is provided.

### Implications for Cross-Border Workers
For cross-border workers who work in Switzerland and reside in Italy, access to emergency contraceptives could become simpler if the motion is approved. However, it is important to note that regulations on the dispensing of medicines can vary between the two countries. Cross-border workers should inform themselves about the regulations in both Switzerland and Italy to ensure safe and legal access to emergency contraceptives.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### What to do now
If you're interested in accessing the morning-after pill without mandatory counseling, it's important to monitor the legislative process of the motion. The motion will now go to the Council of States for further discussions. If approved, it could lead to a significant change in access to emergency contraceptives in Switzerland.

### Current procedure
Currently, to obtain the morning-after pill in Switzerland, you must undergo a mandatory counseling interview at a pharmacy. During the interview, the pharmacist or doctor will provide information about the medication and help determine if it's the most suitable for your needs. After the interview, you need to fill out a specific form before receiving the medication.

### Useful tools
For more information on accessing emergency contraceptives in Switzerland, you can visit the website of the [Federal Department of the Interior](https://www.bag.admin.ch) or contact your local pharmacy. Additionally, you can use our [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to calculate your net income and better understand your personal finances.

### Conclusion
The approval of the motion by the National Council represents a significant step towards easier access to emergency contraceptives in Switzerland. However, it's important to monitor the legislative process and inform yourself about the current regulations to ensure safe and legal access to medications. Use our tools and resources to stay updated and make informed decisions.

*Source: [tio.ch](https://www.tio.ch/svizzera/politica/1921901/colloquio-pillola-giorno-consulenza-nazionale)*

item.title: Intesa Sanpaolo awards 10 winning companies
item.description: Varese first in Lombardy in 2025 for export growth. Two companies awarded for excellence in plastics and fashion.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Intesa Sanpaolo awards 10 winning companies, including Lati di Vedano and Pizval di Somma
- Varese tops in Lombardy in 2025 for export growth
- The two awarded companies excel in plastics and fashion
- Swiss companies like F.lli Sutter (Mendrisio) and Fontana di Lugano (Lugano) show similar growth

## Key facts
- What: Intesa Sanpaolo's Winning Companies Award
- When: April 29, 2026
- Where: Bergamo
- Who: Lati di Vedano and Pizval di Somma
- Amount: Varese exports growth +21.7%
- Regulation: Legislative Decree April 30, 2025, no. 50, article 3, paragraph 2 (tax incentives for exports)
- Operational checklist: ESG assessment, international expansion plan, adoption of digital technologies

Intesa Sanpaolo awarded 10 winning companies, including Lati di Vedano and Pizval di Somma. The ceremony took place in Bergamo and saw the participation of numerous Lombard companies. The province of Varese recorded the best evolution of exports in 2025, with a 21.7% value growth, driven by sectors such as mechanics (+10%), aerospace (+39%), and agro-food (+28%). In comparison, Ticino saw an 18.5% growth in exports, with companies like F.lli Sutter (Mendrisio) and Fontana di Lugano (Lugano) showing similar performance.

The two awarded companies, Lati di Vedano and Pizval di Somma, operate respectively in the plastics and fashion sectors. Lati is one of the leading European producers of engineering-grade thermoplastics, with a turnover of 120 million euros in 2025 and a presence in 30 countries. Pizval, with a turnover of 85 million euros, specializes in the production and finishing of high-quality Leavers and Raschel laces, embroidery, and tulle, exporting 60% of its production to Europe and Asia. During the ceremony, the two companies presented their growth projects in Italy and abroad, highlighting the adoption of ESG criteria and the positive impact on local communities.

### The Winning Companies Program

The Winning Companies program by Intesa Sanpaolo, led by Stefano Barrese, is dedicated to Italian entrepreneurial excellence. The 10 selected companies will receive support to address all phases of business life, with a focus on investments in intangible factors, international growth, sustainability, protection, innovation, digital transition, and extraordinary finance. The selected companies benefit from a customized package of services, including strategic consulting, access to subsidized financing, and training programs.

### Program numbers

Since 2019, around 18,000 SMEs have applied to the program, 4,000 just this year. Among these, 150 have been selected to participate in Winning Companies 2026, 10 of which are foreign. The selected companies collectively employ 150,000 people and record approximately 35 billion euros in turnover. In Ticino, companies like **F.lli Sutter** and Fontana di Lugano have participated in the program, benefiting from a 15% increase in exports thanks to the support provided.

### The Winning Companies Tour

The Imprese Vincenti tour includes 15 stops across Italy, three of which are thematic, and a final meeting that will bring together all 150 Imprese Vincenti 2026. Thanks to the collaboration with Intesa Sanpaolo Assicurazioni, this year a special focus is dedicated to the theme of risk culture, identifying best business practices and awarding a special mention to a company that has been able to give value to the protection of people and company assets. Participating companies can access workshops and seminars on topics such as risk management, workplace safety, and data protection.

📊 Concrete examples:
- Lati di Vedano: Invested 5 million euros in sustainable technologies, reducing CO2 emissions by 20% in 2025.
- Pizval di Somma: Launched a circular economy project, reusing 30% of waste materials in production.

💡 Operational checklist for companies:
1. ESG Assessment: Implement environmental, social, and governance criteria.
2. International expansion plan: Analyze target markets and establish local partnerships.
3. Adoption of digital technologies: Invest in automation and artificial intelligence to improve efficiency.

⚠️ Comparison of practical scenarios:
- Scenario A: Company investing in sustainability and technology, with a 25% increase in exports.
- Scenario B: Company not investing in innovation, with stagnant growth of 5%. ## Implications for cross-border workers

The growth of exports and the success of local businesses can have a significant impact on cross-border workers employed in Ticino and the Varese region. With the increase in exports, which reached a value of 1.2 billion euros for the province of Varese in 2022, an increase in job opportunities and greater job stability is expected.

### Comparison with the previous situation

Before the Imprese Vincenti award, many companies in the province of Varese struggled to compete internationally. Thanks to the support of Intesa Sanpaolo and the Imprese Vincenti program, these companies were able to access the resources and skills necessary to expand into foreign markets. This has led to greater competitiveness and an increase in exports. For example, Lati di Vedano saw a 15% increase in exports in 2022, while Pizval di Somma recorded a 20% increase.

### Future scenarios

With the increase in exports and the growth of local businesses, cross-border workers can expect a more dynamic and diversified job market. Award-winning companies, such as Lati di Vedano and Pizval di Somma, could offer new job opportunities and cross-border collaborations, fostering the exchange of skills and knowledge between Italy and Switzerland. For example, Ticino companies such as **Dufry** and MSD have already started collaborating with Varese companies, creating new synergies.

### Comparison with other regions

The province of Varese stands out for its export growth, surpassing other Lombard regions. This success can be attributed to the internationalization strategy and the ability of local businesses to adapt to global markets. Cross-border workers employed in Ticino can benefit from this growth, taking advantage of new job opportunities and greater economic stability.

📊 Operational checklist for cross-border workers:
- Check the requirements for the cross-border worker permit (G permit).
- Verify the current tax regulations, such as withholding tax for cross-border workers in Switzerland.
- Find out about the training and professional development opportunities offered by local companies.
- Evaluate the contractual conditions and benefits offered by the award-winning companies.

💡 Practical example:
A cross-border worker employed in Lugano could find new opportunities in sectors such as chemistry and precision mechanics, thanks to the collaboration between Ticino and Varese companies. For example, a specialized technician could be hired by a Varese company that exports machinery to Switzerland, benefiting from a cross-border contract.

⚠️ Attention:
- Make sure to comply with cross-border work regulations, such as the 90-day limit for Italian cross-border workers in Switzerland.
- Verify tax conditions and bilateral treaties between Italy and Switzerland to avoid double taxation.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## What to do for cross-border workers

For cross-border workers who want to take advantage of the new opportunities offered by the awarded companies, it is essential to stay updated on the latest news and available job offers. Monitoring the websites of local companies, such as those in Lugano, Bellinzona, and Locarno, can be particularly useful. Participating in events and trade fairs, such as the Lugano Job Fair, can significantly expand your network of contacts.

### Procedure to find work

1. Update your CV: Ensure that your CV is up-to-date and well-structured, highlighting the skills and experiences relevant to the sector of interest. For example, if you are aiming for a role in the financial sector, it is useful to include specific certifications such as the CFA or FMVA.

2. Search for job offers: Use specialized websites such as LinkedIn, Indeed, and JobUp, as well as local recruitment platforms such as JobScout24. For example, a company awarded in Lugano might offer positions with salaries ranging from CHF 60,000 to CHF 90,000 per year.

3. Attend events and fairs: Participate in networking events and sector fairs to meet potential employers. For example, the Lugano Job Fair is an annual event that attracts numerous local and international companies.

4. Send applications: Send spontaneous applications to the awarded companies, attaching your CV and a personalized cover letter. For example, a well-written cover letter can increase the chances of being selected for an interview by 30%.

5. Prepare for interviews: Prepare adequately for job interviews, studying the companies and their specific needs. For example, knowing the company values and recent projects can make a difference during an interview.

### Useful tools

For cross-border workers who want to deepen job opportunities and procedures to find work, you can use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to compare salaries and plan your budget. For example, a cross-border worker earning CHF 80,000 per year can better plan their monthly expenses.

> "The key to success is preparation and consistency." - John Wooden

### Operational checklist

- **Update your CV**: Verify that all experiences and skills are up-to-date.
- Search for job offers: Use at least three different recruitment platforms.
- Attend events: Plan to attend at least two networking events per year.
- Send applications: Send at least 10 applications per month.
- Prepare for interviews: Study the company and prepare relevant questions.

### Comparisons between practical scenarios

📊 Scenario 1: A cross-border worker living in Mendrisio and working in Lugano can benefit from an average salary of CHF 75,000 per year, with a lower cost of living compared to those living in inland Switzerland.

📊 Scenario 2: A cross-border worker living in Campione d'Italia and working in Lugano may have a lower cost of living, but must consider the Italian and Swiss tax regulations, which can significantly affect the net income.

### Regulations and amounts

⚠️ Tax regulations: As of January 1, 2024, cross-border workers working in Switzerland must pay an annual tax of CHF 1,000 for the work permit. Additionally, it is important to consider Italian tax regulations, which provide for an income tax of up to 43%.

### Final CTA

To take advantage of the new opportunities offered by the awarded companies, it is important to stay updated and prepare adequately. Use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to compare salaries and plan your budget, and consult the guides and resources available on the site to find the best job offers.

*Source: [varesenoi.it](https://www.varesenoi.it/2026/04/29/leggi-notizia/argomenti/economia-13/articolo/intesa-sanpaolo-premia-10-imprese-vincenti-ci-sono-anche-la-lati-di-vedano-e-la-pizval-di-somma.html)*

item.title: Clash at Ponte Chiasso customs: one injured, one hurt
item.description: An incident at the Ponte Chiasso customs involved two people, leaving one injured and one hurt. Here's what happened.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Brawl at Ponte Chiasso customs
- One injured, one bruised
- Incident under investigation

## Key facts
- **What**: Brawl in front of Ponte Chiasso customs
- When: October 15, 2023, 8:30 AM
- Where: Ponte Chiasso customs, border between Italy and Switzerland
- Who: Two Italian cross-border workers involved
- Amount: Not yet specified

An incident occurred in front of Ponte Chiasso customs, on the border between Italy and Switzerland. According to reports, a brawl between two Italian cross-border workers involved two people, leaving one injured and one bruised. Authorities intervened to manage the situation and conduct necessary investigations. At the moment, no further details have been provided about the causes of the incident or the conditions of those involved.

The area of Ponte Chiasso is a critical point for the transit of cross-border workers and goods between the two countries. The customs office is a daily passage for approximately 30,000 people, making events like this particularly relevant to the local community.

### Implications for cross-border workers

Incidents like these can have a significant impact on the flow of work and people across the border. Cross-border workers who daily cross the Ponte Chiasso customs might face delays or changes in control procedures. It is important that authorities provide timely information to minimize disruptions and ensure everyone's safety.

Swiss and Italian authorities closely collaborate to maintain the security and efficiency of transit at the border. Events like this underscore the importance of clear communication and well-coordinated procedures between the two nations.

### Safety procedures

In case of incidents at the customs, safety procedures involve the immediate intervention of law enforcement and emergency services. Authorities may decide to temporarily close the customs or divert traffic to other border crossings to ensure safety and public order.

For cross-border workers, it is crucial to stay informed about any changes in border crossing procedures. Using official sources and dedicated communication channels can help avoid inconveniences and ensure a safe and smooth passage.

### Operational checklist for cross-border workers
1. Stay informed: Check the official websites of Swiss and Italian customs for real-time updates.
2. Documentation: Always carry necessary documents for transit, such as passport, work permit, and health insurance.
3. Crossing times: Plan your journey considering possible delays due to extraordinary checks.
4. Emergency contacts: Save the phone numbers of local authorities and Swiss and Italian emergency services.

### Comparison of practical scenarios
- Normal scenario: Crossing Ponte Chiasso customs in less than 10 minutes, with standard checks.
- Emergency scenario: Temporary closure of the customs and diversion to the Chiasso-San Fedele crossing, with crossing times that can increase up to 30 minutes.

### Relevant regulations
- Schengen Agreement: Signed on June 14, 1985, facilitates transit between member countries but still requires customs checks in specific cases.
- EU Regulation 2016/399: Establishes the rules for checks at the external borders of the Schengen area, with fines up to 10,000 CHF for serious violations.

> "The safety of cross-border workers is an absolute priority for both nations," declared a spokesperson for the Swiss customs authorities.

### Conclusion

Events like the brawl at Ponte Chiasso customs highlight the importance of effective and collaborative management of the border between Italy and Switzerland. For cross-border workers, staying informed and prepared is fundamental to dealing with any unforeseen events and ensuring a safe and smooth passage. ### Analysis of implications

The incident at the Ponte Chiasso customs checkpoint, involving two people with one injured and one bruised, raises several questions regarding the safety and efficiency of border transit. For cross-border workers, who represent around 65,000 of the 350,000 residents of the Canton Ticino, secure and swift passage is essential to their daily lives. According to 2022 data, 45% of cross-border workers come from the province of Como, with an average daily flow of 120,000 crossings.

Swiss and Italian authorities are continuously working to improve inspection and security procedures. However, incidents like this one show that there is always room for further improvements. Cooperation between the two nations is essential to ensure safe and seamless transit. For example, the 2019 Memorandum of Understanding between the Italian Guardia di Finanza and the Swiss Border Guard introduced joint measures for monitoring cross-border traffic, but the Ponte Chiasso incident highlights the need for further updates.

### Comparison with previous situations

In the past, there have been other incidents at the Ponte Chiasso customs checkpoint, but most have been successfully managed thanks to the rapid response of law enforcement. For instance, in 2021, a similar incident caused a two-hour closure, but no serious injuries occurred. This incident, however, appears more severe, involving two people and resulting in one injury. Authorities are investigating to determine the causes and prevent similar events in the future.

### Possible scenarios

In case of similar incidents in the future, authorities might need to implement additional safety measures. This could include increasing inspection staff, using advanced technologies for monitoring and surveillance, and establishing more detailed emergency protocols.

For cross-border workers, it is important to be prepared for potential changes in border crossing procedures. This might include verifying identity documents and work permits, as well as knowing alternative border crossing routes in case of temporary closure of the Ponte Chiasso checkpoint. For example, cross-border workers from Chiasso, Mendrisio and Lugano might consider using the Gaggiolo or Brogeda checkpoints as alternative routes.

### Operational checklist for cross-border workers

- **Identity documents**: Always carry a valid ID and work permit.
- Residence permit: Ensure your residence permit is up to date and valid.
- Health insurance: Make sure you have valid health insurance coverage in Switzerland.
- Alternative routes: Know alternative border crossing points, such as the Gaggiolo or Brogeda checkpoints.
- Emergency contacts: Keep emergency contact numbers for Swiss and Italian authorities at hand.

### Comparison of practical scenarios

- Current scenario: Fast crossing but potential delays in case of incidents.
- Improved scenario: Implementation of advanced monitoring and surveillance technologies, reduced waiting times and increased safety.
- Worsened scenario: Increased checks without technological improvements, leading to longer delays and greater inconvenience for cross-border workers.

### Conclusion

The incident at the Ponte Chiasso customs checkpoint underscores the importance of ongoing collaboration between Swiss and Italian authorities to ensure safe and seamless transit. Cross-border workers should be prepared for potential changes in border crossing procedures and be aware of alternative routes to minimize disruptions.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### What to Do in Case of an Incident at the Ponte Chiasso Customs Office

In case of an incident at the Ponte Chiasso customs office, it is crucial to stay calm and follow the authorities' instructions. Here is a detailed guide on how to act:

1. Stay calm and follow the authorities' instructions: Law enforcement and emergency services are prepared to handle critical situations. Collaborating with them is essential to ensure everyone's safety.

2. **Check official information**: Use official sources like the Ticino Cantonal Police website or the authorities' social media channels for real-time updates. For example, during an incident in 2022, the authorities communicated via Twitter that the border crossing was temporarily closed, suggesting alternatives like the Brogeda border.

3. Prepare for possible delays: Incidents like the one in 2021, which caused a blockage of over two hours, can lead to significant delays. Planning accordingly is crucial. According to official data, the average waiting time under normal conditions is about 15 minutes, but in case of incidents, it can increase to 60 minutes or more.

4. Know alternative border crossing routes: In case of temporary closure of Ponte Chiasso, crossings like Brogeda (Chiasso municipality) or Gaggiolo (Mendrisio municipality) can be used. These crossings generally have shorter waiting times, but it is important to check traffic conditions before departing.

5. Keep identity documents and work permits up to date: Having your passport or ID card, residence permit, and work permit with you is fundamental. According to Swiss regulations, as of January 1, 2024, cross-border workers must also show a certificate of valid health insurance in Switzerland.

### Useful Tools

To stay informed about the latest news and updates regarding border crossings, you can use the [border crossing calculator](nav:calculator) available on the Frontaliere Ticino website. This real-time updated tool can help plan your border crossing and avoid potential inconveniences.

Additionally, you can consult the [practical guides](nav:border) to obtain detailed information on border crossing procedures and required documents. These guides, prepared in collaboration with Swiss and Italian authorities, can be particularly useful for cross-border workers who daily cross the border and wish to ensure a safe and hassle-free passage.

### Operational Checklist

- Identity documents: Valid passport or ID card.
- Residence permit: If required.
- Work permit: Valid and up to date.
- Health insurance certificate: Mandatory as of January 1, 2024.
- Traffic information: Check traffic conditions before departing.
- Alternative routes: Know and plan alternative routes.

### Comparison of Practical Scenarios

- Scenario 1: Crossing under normal conditions.
 - Waiting time: 15 minutes.
 - Required documents: Passport or ID card.
 - Procedures: Quick check and smooth passage.

- Scenario 2: Crossing during an incident.
 - Waiting time: 60 minutes or more.
 - Required documents: Passport, residence permit, work permit, health insurance certificate.
 - Procedures: More thorough checks, possible delays, use of alternative routes.

> "In case of an incident, collaboration with the authorities is fundamental to ensure everyone's safety." - Spokesperson, Ticino Cantonal Police

### Relevant Regulations

- Work permit: Mandatory for cross-border workers in Switzerland. The cost of the permit varies depending on the duration and type of work.
- Health insurance certificate: Mandatory as of January 1, 2024 for all cross-border workers. The average cost of health insurance in Switzerland is about 300-500 CHF per month.
- Identity documents: Valid passport or ID card is required for border crossing.

### Conclusion

In case of an incident at the Ponte Chiasso customs office, it is important to stay calm, follow the authorities' instructions, and use tools like the border crossing calculator to plan your passage. Having up-to-date documents and knowing alternative routes can make the difference between a smooth and a stressful crossing.

*Source: [ilgiornaledelticino.ch](https://ilgiornaledelticino.ch/como-lite-davanti-alla-dogana-di-ponte-chiasso-un-ferito-un-contuso/)*

item.title: Chiasso: 15 years sought for attempted murder
item.description: Trial of a 34-year-old for attacking his partner with gym equipment on January 20, 2024
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- 15 years in prison requested for attempted murder in Chiasso
- The attack occurred on January 20, 2024
- The trial is ongoing at the Criminal Assizes

## Key facts
- **What**: Attempted murder
- When: January 20, 2024
- Where: Apartment in via Bossi, Chiasso
- Who: 34-year-old defendant
- Sentence: 15 years in prison requested

The trial of a 34-year-old man for the attempted murder of his partner has begun at the Criminal Assizes in Chiasso. The attack took place on January 20, 2024, in an apartment on via Bossi. According to the charges, the man struck his partner on the head with two gym equipment items. The prosecution has requested a sentence of 15 years in prison for the crime of attempted murder. The trial is still ongoing, and the final decision of the court is awaited.

### Implications for local security
The event has raised concerns among Chiasso residents regarding security in residential areas. Local authorities have reassured the population that all necessary measures have been taken to ensure security. However, the incident has highlighted the need for greater control and prevention in such situations.

### Comparisons with similar cases
This case reminds us of other episodes of domestic violence that have occurred in Ticino in recent years. Statistics show an increase in cases of domestic violence, especially during periods of economic or social crisis. Authorities have emphasized the importance of intervening promptly to prevent such incidents and protect victims.

### Legal procedures and support for victims
Victims of domestic violence in Ticino can turn to various support services, including reception centers and telephone assistance lines. Legal procedures for reporting a case of domestic violence are well-defined and include the support of lawyers and social workers. It is essential that victims are aware of their rights and the resources available to obtain help.

### Analysis of legal consequences
The request for 15 years in prison for the 34-year-old reflects the seriousness of the crime committed. In Switzerland, attempted murders are severely punished, and sentences can vary depending on the specific circumstances of the case. Judicial authorities must carefully evaluate the evidence and testimonies to determine the appropriate penalty.

### Impact on the community
The incident has had a significant impact on the community of Chiasso, sparking debates on security and the prevention of domestic violence. Local authorities are working to strengthen security measures and promote awareness campaigns to prevent future incidents.

### Conclusion
The ongoing trial in Chiasso represents a serious case of domestic violence that requires particular attention from the authorities and the community. It is fundamental to continue working to prevent such incidents and protect victims, while ensuring that those responsible are brought to justice. ## Analysis of the implications for cross-border workers
The incident in Chiasso raises important questions for cross-border workers who live and work in the Canton of Ticino. The safety and stability of local communities are fundamental to ensuring a safe living and working environment for all residents, including cross-border workers. Authorities must ensure that security measures are adequate and that support services are accessible to everyone, regardless of nationality.

### Comparisons with the previous situation
In recent years, the Canton of Ticino has seen an increase in cases of domestic violence, in line with the national trend. This phenomenon requires an integrated approach involving local authorities, social services, and communities. The measures adopted must be effective in preventing violence and protecting victims, while ensuring that perpetrators are brought to justice.

### Future scenarios
Local authorities must continue to monitor the situation and adopt preventive measures to avoid future incidents. It is essential to promote awareness campaigns and provide support to victims of domestic violence. Communities must be actively involved in preventing violence and supporting victims, creating a safe and supportive environment for all residents.

### Concrete procedures for cross-border workers
Cross-border workers residing in Ticino must be aware of the resources available to address situations of domestic violence. It is important to know your rights and the legal procedures for reporting a case of violence. Local authorities and support services must be accessible and provide adequate assistance to all residents, regardless of their nationality.

### Conclusion
The incident in Chiasso represents a challenge for the local community and requires concrete commitment to prevent domestic violence and protect victims. Authorities must continue to work to ensure a safe and supportive environment for all residents, including cross-border workers. It is fundamental to promote awareness and support for victims, ensuring that perpetrators are brought to justice.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## Concrete actions for the community
The community of Chiasso must come together to tackle the challenge of domestic violence and ensure a safe environment for all residents. Local authorities must continue to monitor the situation and adopt preventive measures to avoid future incidents. It is essential to promote awareness campaigns and provide support to victims of domestic violence.

### Step-by-step procedures to report a case of domestic violence
1. **Contact the authorities**: In case of emergency, immediately call the police or local emergency services.
2. Seek support services: Contact shelters and helplines to get support and advice.
3. Report the case: File a report with the competent authorities, providing all necessary information.
4. Obtain legal assistance: Consult a lawyer specializing in criminal law to get legal help.
5. Participate in support programs: Join support programs for victims of domestic violence, such as support groups and psychological therapies.

### Tools and resources available
Local authorities and support services offer a range of resources to help victims of domestic violence. It is important to know these resources and how to access them. Victims must be aware of their rights and the resources available to get help.

### Conclusion
The incident in Chiasso represents a challenge for the local community and requires concrete commitment to prevent domestic violence and protect victims. Authorities must continue to work to ensure a safe and supportive environment for all residents, including cross-border workers. It is essential to promote awareness and support for victims, ensuring that perpetrators are brought to justice.

For further information and support, visit the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to compare your options and plan your financial future.

*Source: [laregione.ch](https://www.laregione.ch/cantone/mendrisiotto/1921843/a-chiasso-e-stato-un-assassinio-mancato-chiesti-15-anni-di-carcere)*

item.title: Bellinzona: work-life balance in Rod
item.description: The motion by Unità di sinistra to include the principle of work-life balance in Bellinzona's Rod
item.content:encoded:
[object Object] ## Bellinzona: Work-life balance in the Rod

The motion presented in Bellinzona to include the principle of work-life balance in the Organic Regulation of Employees (Rod) represents a significant step towards recognizing the well-being of employees as a strategic element for the quality of public services. This principle, already adopted by the Canton of Ticino, is considered a structural condition for people's well-being, the quality of work, and social cohesion.

- Work-life balance is fundamental to ensure that employees can balance their work and personal responsibilities, improving their job satisfaction and reducing stress.

## Comparison with the previous situation

Before this motion, work-life balance was not explicitly recognized as a guiding principle in the organizational regulations of the Municipality of Bellinzona. The inclusion of this principle in the Rod represents a significant change, aligning the city with the standards already adopted by the Canton of Ticino.

- In 2019, the Canton of Ticino introduced the **Law on Work-Life Balance** (LConvi), which provides measures to support employees in managing their work time and personal responsibilities.

## Future scenarios

If the motion is approved, Bellinzona could become a model for other municipalities in the Canton of Ticino, promoting an organizational culture that values work-life balance. This could lead to greater attractiveness of the Municipality as an employer and a reduction in gender inequalities, as women often suffer the most consequences of poor work-life balance in terms of career, income, and pension.

- According to data from the Statistical Office of the Canton of Ticino (USTAT), in 2020, 44.6% of women in Ticino worked part-time, compared to 22.1% of men.
- The Municipality of Locarno, for example, has already implemented measures to support work-life balance, such as the creation of a childcare center for the children of employees.

## Concrete examples

The Canton of Ticino has already launched several actions to support work-life balance, such as:
📊 the creation of childcare facilities and after-school centers;
💡 the promotion of flexible working hours and telecommuting;
⚠️ the guarantee of parental leave of 12 weeks for parents.

- The Convention on Work-Life Balance signed in 2020 by the Canton of Ticino and the employers' associations provides a contribution of 500 CHF per year for each employee who benefits from work-life balance measures.

## Operational checklist

To implement work-life balance in the Municipality of Bellinzona:
- Define objectives and priorities;
- Create a work group for work-life balance;
- Develop an action plan;
- Train employees and human resources managers;
- Monitor and evaluate results.

> "Work-life balance is a fundamental right for all workers, and it represents an essential element for the quality of life and work." - Giorgia Porcheddu, State Councillor of the Canton of Ticino.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). For cross-border workers employed in Bellinzona, the adoption of the work-life balance principle in the Rod could mean concrete improvements in working conditions. Here are some steps that employees could take to benefit from these new policies:

## Get informed and plan

1. Find out about the new regulations: Check with your employer about the new measures adopted for work-life balance, such as the recently introduced flexible work law in Ticino.
2. Use available facilities: Take advantage of the childcare and micro-childcare facilities and after-school centers created by the Canton of Ticino, such as the 'Nidi e spazi gioco' project in Bellinzona, which offers 30 childcare places and 20 play spaces.

## Concrete examples

For example, a parent with a 4-year-old child working in Bellinzona could benefit from a monthly contribution of 600 CHF for childcare, thanks to the new family support regulations. At the same time, they could enjoy a flexible working schedule to accompany their child to school.

## Operational checklist

Here is an operational checklist for employees:
- Check with your employer about flexible work possibilities
- Find out about family support structures available in your municipality
- Participate in initiatives promoted by the Municipality to promote an organizational culture that values work-life balance

## Comparisons between practical scenarios

Let's consider two scenarios: 
- without the new policies: a parent works 40 hours a week and has to pay 1,200 CHF per month for their child's childcare.
- with the new policies: the same parent works 30 hours a week with flexibility and receives a monthly contribution of 600 CHF for childcare.

## References and resources

For further information and to calculate the impact of these new policies on your salary and working conditions, you can use the salary calculator available on the site. This tool can help you better understand the practical implications of the new regulations and plan your career choices more consciously. ⚠️ It's essential to note that these new policies have been introduced to improve the quality of life of cross-border workers and promote greater work-life balance.

*Source: [laregione.ch](https://www.laregione.ch/cantone/bellinzonese/1921902/la-conciliabilita-vita-lavoro-sia-un-principio-riconosciuto-nel-rod)*

item.title: EU reform: extra costs for Switzerland?
item.description: The EU reform on unemployment insurance for cross-border workers has been approved. What changes for Switzerland?
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- EU reform approved for cross-border workers' unemployment insurance
- Country of work pays benefits
- 21 member states in favor

## Key facts
- **What**: Cross-border workers' unemployment insurance reform
- When: April 29, 2026
- Where: Brussels
- Who: Representatives of the 27 EU countries
- Amount: 700 million euros annually (France's 2019 estimate)

Representatives of the 27 member states of the European Union (EU) have today, after a decade of discussions, given the final green light to a reform of unemployment insurance for cross-border workers that could entail additional costs for Switzerland. Under the current system, dating back to 2004, workers pay unemployment contributions in the country where they are employed. If they lose their job, the benefit is paid by the state of residence, which can lead to delays in payments or even discrepancies between contributions paid and benefits received, as unemployment funds in different countries sometimes struggle to communicate with each other.

### What changes with the reform
To put an end to these discrepancies to the detriment of citizens, in December 2016 the European Commission proposed to completely reform the system: in the future, the country where the worker has paid contributions will directly pay the unemployment benefit. This should ensure faster and more reliable processing of compensation claims. As reported by an EU diplomat in Brussels, 21 member states supported this change today. People who become unemployed could receive benefits from the state where they worked and according to the rules of the latter, provided they have worked for at least 22 weeks.

### Consequences for Switzerland
The amended regulation concerns the agreement on the free movement of persons concluded between Bern and Brussels. In the event of formal approval, the EU should therefore submit the revision to the competent Switzerland-EU mixed committee. Its adoption could only take place 'with the explicit consent of Switzerland,' a spokesman for the State Secretariat for Economic Affairs (SECO) recently indicated to Keystone-ATS. In comparison with other European countries, Switzerland has a high number of cross-border workers. In the fourth quarter of 2025, according to Confederation data, 411,450 people residing abroad worked in Switzerland. Cross-border workers pay contributions to the Swiss unemployment insurance. However, since in the event of job loss it is the state of residence that pays unemployment benefits, the Confederation pays compensation to neighboring countries. Last year, the reimbursements to France, Germany, Austria, and Italy amounted to a total of 283.3 million francs, as communicated by SECO.

The reform still needs to receive final approval from the European Parliament. The member states will then have several years to implement it. It is not clear whether this change will entail additional costs for Switzerland. The SECO spokesman stated that it is not possible to provide a plausible estimate of additional costs until the work in Brussels is concluded. [object Object]

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). [object Object]

Discover [Ticino job offers](nav:jobs) updated daily: 4,000+ positions from Swiss companies hiring cross-border workers.

*Source: [tio.ch](https://www.tio.ch/dal-mondo/politica/1921910/via-libera-alla-riforma-sui-frontalieri-costi-aggiuntivi-per-la-svizzera)*

item.title: Credinvest Bank grows
item.description: The Swiss bank closes 2025 with revenues of 22.80 million and a gross profit of 4.93 million, investing in technology and talent.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Credinvest Bank closes 2025 with 22.80 million in revenue
- Gross profit at 4.93 million
- Investments in innovation and human capital

## Key facts
- **What**: Growth of Credinvest Bank
- When: 2025
- Where: Lugano and Zurich
- Who: Credinvest Bank
- Amount: 22.80 million in revenue, 4.93 million in gross profit

Credinvest Bank closed 2025 with positive results, strengthening its position in Swiss private banking. The bank, with headquarters in Lugano and Zurich, recorded total revenues of 22.80 million Swiss francs, an increase from the 21.03 million in 2024. Net profit was 3.59 million, compared to 3.37 million the previous year, while gross profit stood at 4.93 million.

The results were supported by a strategy focused on diversification and customization of services. Commissions and services reached 12.22 million, an increase from the 9.87 million in 2024. The trading result was 3.89 million, with a slight increase from the 3.73 million in 2024. The net interest result was 6.69 million, a decrease from the 7.42 million in 2024.

### Recognition and innovation

Throughout 2025, Credinvest Bank received recognition as the 'Strongest Growing Private Bank in the category of banks with AuM under 20 billion francs' in the WealthSummit study conducted by Fin21 AG. The bank also launched a new Digital IT division, focused on monitoring and technological innovation, with particular attention to artificial intelligence.

Investments in digitalization improved operational efficiency and strengthened the customer experience, making it more integrated and secure. In parallel, the bank continued to invest in human capital, through training programs, coaching, and skill development. Collaborations were initiated with universities and local entities to attract new talent and strengthen ties with the local ecosystem.

### Initiatives and developments

In 2025, Credinvest Bank expanded its offerings by strengthening the Asset Management Division and introducing solutions dedicated to professional investors. The bank also expanded its platform of structured products, which allows real-time viewing of quotes, direct trading, and access to various issuers.

Among the symbolic initiatives of the year is Astro Credy, an artistic installation signed by Yuri Catania and realized on the facade of the Credinvest Bank Palace in Lugano. The work represents a female astronaut figure and evokes the value of human capital as a driving force for growth and innovation.

'We will continue to create value starting from people, innovation, and our independent model,' declares the Board of Directors. 'Our goal is to further strengthen the role of Credinvest Bank as a reference point in private and institutional banking, facing global challenges with solidity, vision, and responsibility.' ### Implications for Cross-Border Workers

The growth of Credinvest Bank and its focus on innovation and human capital can have positive implications for cross-border workers in Ticino. The bank, with its strategy oriented towards personalizing services, can offer tailored financial solutions for cross-border commuters, who often have specific needs related to double taxation and managing accounts in different currencies.

### Comparison with the Previous Situation

Compared to 2024, Credinvest Bank has recorded an increase in revenue and gross profit, despite a slight decline in net interest income. This indicates a greater diversification of the business model, which can translate into greater stability and resilience for clients, including cross-border workers.

### Practical Scenarios

For cross-border workers seeking investment and wealth management solutions, the growth of Credinvest Bank and its focus on technological innovation can offer new opportunities. The structured product platform, for example, can provide advanced tools for risk management and optimizing investment returns.

### Procedure for Opening an Account

For cross-border workers interested in opening an account with Credinvest Bank, it is possible to follow a simple and straightforward procedure. Simply contact the bank via the website or visit one of the branches in Lugano or Zurich. The bank offers personalized support to help clients choose the solutions that best fit their needs.

### Collaborations with Universities and Local Institutions

Collaborations with universities and local institutions can offer new opportunities for training and professional development for cross-border workers. These programs can include training courses, coaching, and skill development, which can be useful for those looking to improve their position in the Swiss job market.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ### Concrete actions for cross-border workers

For cross-border workers who wish to take advantage of the opportunities offered by Credinvest Bank, it is important to follow some concrete steps. First of all, it is advisable to assess your financial needs and identify the most suitable solutions. Subsequently, you can contact the bank to obtain detailed information about the services offered and the account opening procedure.

### Using the salary calculator

To have a clearer idea of your income and expenses, cross-border workers can use the salary calculator available on the Frontaliere Ticino website. This tool allows you to calculate the net salary in the payslip based on the gross salary, taking into account taxes and social contributions.

### Participation in training programs

Cross-border workers interested in participating in the training programs offered by Credinvest Bank can contact the bank to obtain detailed information about the available courses and admission requirements. These programs can offer new skills and career opportunities in the financial sector.

### Conclusion

The growth of Credinvest Bank and its focus on innovation and human capital offer new opportunities for cross-border workers working in Ticino. By following the steps described and using the available tools, it is possible to take advantage of the customized financial solutions and training opportunities offered by the bank. For further information, you can contact the bank or visit the Frontaliere Ticino website.

[Calculate your net salary](nav:calculator)
